-lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,360p' harness/models.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Core data structures for the autonomous harness.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\\\\nfrom enum import Enum\\\\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass TaskStatus(str, Enum):\\\\n    PENDING = \\\\\\\"pending\\\\\\\"\\\\n    PLANNING = \\\\\\\"planning\\\\\\\"\\\\n    EXECUTING = \\\\\\\"executing\\\\\\\"\\\\n    PAUSED_QUOTA = \\\\\\\"paused_quota\\\\\\\"\\\\n    PAUSED_PERMISSION = \\\\\\\"paused_permission\\\\\\\"\\\\n    DONE = \\\\\\\"done\\\\\\\"\\\\n    FAILED = \\\\\\\"failed\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass StageStatus(str, Enum):\\\\n    PENDING = \\\\\\\"pending\\\\\\\"\\\\n    RUNNING = \\\\\\\"running\\\\\\\"\\\\n    DONE = \\\\\\\"done\\\\\\\"\\\\n    FAILED = \\\\\\\"failed\\\\\\\"\\\\n    SKIPPED = \\\\\\\"skipped\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass RiskLevel(str, Enum):\\\\n    SAFE = \\\\\\\"safe\\\\\\\"\\\\n    MODERATE = \\\\\\\"moderate\\\\\\\"\\\\n    DANGEROUS = \\\\\\\"dangerous\\\\\\\"\\\\n    CRITICAL = \\\\\\\"critical\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n@dataclass(slots=True)\\\\nclass PlannedStage:\\\\n    stage_type: str\\\\n    stage_order: int\\\\n    model_role: str\\\\n    verify_cmd: Optional[str] = None\\\\n    metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n@dataclass(slots=True)\\\\nclass TaskRecord:\\\\n    task_id: str\\\\n    title: str\\\\n    description: str\\\\n    goal: Optional[str]\\\\n    verify_cmd: Optional[str]\\\\n    complexity: str\\\\n    priority: int\\\\n    status: TaskStatus\\\\n    working_dir: Optional[str]\\\\n    created_at: str\\\\n    updated_at: str\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n@dataclass(slots=True)\\\\nclass StageRecord:\\\\n    stage_id: str\\\\n    task_id: str\\\\n    stage_type: str\\\\n    stage_order: int\\\\n    model_role: str\\\\n    assigned_model: Optional[str]\\\\n    assigned_provider: Optional[str]\\\\n    status: StageStatus\\\\n    handoff_doc_path: Optional[str]\\\\n    result_summary: Optional[str]\\\\n    token_used: int\\\\n    duration_sec: float\\\\n    started_at: Optional[str]\\\\n    finished_at: Optional[str]\\\\n    retry_count: int = 0\\\\n    verify_cmd: Optional[str] = None\\\\n    metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n@dataclass(slots=True)\\\\nclass PermissionRequestRecord:\\\\n    request_id: str\\\\n    stage_id: str\\\\n    action: str\\\\n    context: Optional[str]\\\\n    risk_level: RiskLevel\\\\n    decision: Optional[str]\\\\n    voters: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)\\\\n    decided_at: Optional[str] = None\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n@dataclass(slots=True)\\\\nclass QuotaEventRecord:\\\\n    event_id: str\\\\n    provider: str\\\\n    account_email: Optional[str]\\\\n    event_type: str\\\\n -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,360p' harness/db.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"SQLite schema and connection helpers for the harness.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nimport sqlite3\\\\nfrom contextlib import contextmanager\\\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\\\nfrom typing import Iterator\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nSCHEMA = \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nPRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (\\\\n    task_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    title TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    description TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    goal TEXT,\\\\n    verify_cmd TEXT,\\\\n    complexity TEXT DEFAULT 'medium',\\\\n    priority INTEGER DEFAULT 50,\\\\n    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',\\\\n    working_dir TEXT,\\\\n    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stages (\\\\n    stage_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    task_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(task_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\\\n    stage_type TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    stage_order INTEGER NOT NULL,\\\\n    model_role TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    assigned_model TEXT,\\\\n    assigned_provider TEXT,\\\\n    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',\\\\n    handoff_doc_path TEXT,\\\\n    result_summary TEXT,\\\\n    token_used INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\\\n    duration_sec REAL DEFAULT 0,\\\\n    started_at TEXT,\\\\n    finished_at TEXT,\\\\n    retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\\\n    verify_cmd TEXT,\\\\n    metadata TEXT DEFAULT '{}'\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS permission_requests (\\\\n    request_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    stage_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES stages(stage_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\\\n    action TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    context TEXT,\\\\n    risk_level TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    decision TEXT,\\\\n    voters TEXT DEFAULT '[]',\\\\n    decided_at TEXT\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS quota_events (\\\\n    event_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    provider TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    account_email TEXT,\\\\n    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    details TEXT DEFAULT '{}',\\\\n    created_at TEXT NOT NULL\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_status_priority\\\\n    ON tasks(status, priority DESC, created_at ASC);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stages_task_order\\\\n    ON stages(task_id, stage_order ASC);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stages_task_status\\\\n    ON stages(task_id, status, stage_order ASC);\\\\n\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass HarnessDB:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Small wrapper around a SQLite database file.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def __init__(self, db_path: str | Path) -> None:\\\\n        self.db_path = Path(db_path)\\\\n        self.db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\\\n        self.initialize()\\\\n\\\\n    def initialize(self) -> None:\\\\n        with self.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.executescript(SCHEMA)\\\\n\\\\n    @contextmanager\\\\n    def connect(self) -> Iterator[sqlite3.Connection]:\\\\n        conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)\\\\n        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row\\\\n        conn.execute(\\\\\\\"PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON\\\\\\\")\\\\n        try:\\\\n            yield conn\\\\n            conn.commit()\\\\n        except Exception:\\\\n            conn.rollback()\\\\n            raise\\\\n        finally:\\\\n            conn.close()\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_17\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,360p' harness/task_queue.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":null,\\\"status\\\":\\\"in_progress\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_17\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,360p' harness/task_queue.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"SQLite-backed task and stage queue.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nimport json\\\\nimport uuid\\\\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\\\\nfrom typing import Iterable, Optional\\\\n\\\\nfrom harness.db import HarnessDB\\\\nfrom harness.models import (\\\\n    PermissionRequestRecord,\\\\n    PlannedStage,\\\\n    QuotaEventRecord,\\\\n    RiskLevel,\\\\n    StageRecord,\\\\n    StageStatus,\\\\n    TaskRecord,\\\\n    TaskStatus,\\\\n)\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef utcnow_iso() -> str:\\\\n    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass TaskQueue:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"CRUD layer for tasks, stages, and audit tables.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def __init__(self, db: HarnessDB) -> None:\\\\n        self.db = db\\\\n\\\\n    def create_task(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        title: str,\\\\n        description: str,\\\\n        *,\\\\n        goal: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n        verify_cmd: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n        complexity: str = \\\\\\\"medium\\\\\\\",\\\\n        priority: int = 50,\\\\n        status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.PENDING,\\\\n        working_dir: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n        task_id: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n    ) -> TaskRecord:\\\\n        now = utcnow_iso()\\\\n        record = TaskRecord(\\\\n            task_id=task_id or uuid.uuid4().hex,\\\\n            title=title,\\\\n            description=description,\\\\n            goal=goal,\\\\n            verify_cmd=verify_cmd,\\\\n            complexity=complexity,\\\\n            priority=priority,\\\\n            status=status,\\\\n            working_dir=working_dir,\\\\n            created_at=now,\\\\n            updated_at=now,\\\\n        )\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                INSERT INTO tasks (\\\\n                    task_id, title, description, goal, verify_cmd, complexity,\\\\n                    priority, status, working_dir, created_at, updated_at\\\\n                ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (\\\\n                    record.task_id,\\\\n                    record.title,\\\\n                    record.description,\\\\n                    record.goal,\\\\n                    record.verify_cmd,\\\\n                    record.complexity,\\\\n                    record.priority,\\\\n                    record.status.value,\\\\n                    record.working_dir,\\\\n                    record.created_at,\\\\n                    record.updated_at,\\\\n                ),\\\\n            )\\\\n        return record\\\\n\\\\n    def get_task(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[TaskRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\", (task_id,)).fetchone()\\\\n        return self._row_to_task(row) if row else None\\\\n\\\\n    def list_tasks(self, status: Optional[TaskStatus] = None) -> list[TaskRecord]:\\\\n        query = \\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM tasks\\\\\\\"\\\\n        params: tuple[object, ...] = ()\\\\n        if status is not None:\\\\n            query += \\\\\\\" WHERE status = ?\\\\\\\"\\\\n            params = (status.value,)\\\\n        query += \\\\\\\" ORDER BY priority DESC, created_at ASC\\\\\\\"\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            rows = conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()\\\\n        return [self._row_to_task(row) for row in rows]\\\\n\\\\n    def pick_next_runnable_task(self) -> Optional[TaskRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                SELECT * FROM tasks\\\\n                WHERE status IN (?, ?, ?)\\\\n                ORDER BY\\\\n                    CASE status\\\\n                        WHEN ? THEN 0\\\\n                        WHEN ? THEN 1\\\\n                        ELSE 2\\\\n                    END,\\\\n                    priority DESC,\\\\n                    created_at ASC\\\\n                LIMIT 1\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (\\\\n                    TaskStatus.EXECUTING.value,\\\\n                    TaskStatus.PENDING.value,\\\\n                    TaskStatus.PLANNING.value,\\\\n                    TaskStatus.EXECUTING.value,\\\\n                    TaskStatus.PENDING.value,\\\\n                ),\\\\n            ).fetchone()\\\\n        return self._row_to_task(row) if row else None\\\\n\\\\n    def update_task_status(self, task_id: str, status: TaskStatus) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"UPDATE tasks SET status = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (status.value, utcnow_iso(), task_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def update_task_working_dir(self, task_id: str, working_dir: str) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"UPDATE tasks SET working_dir = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (working_dir, utcnow_iso(), task_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def save_stages(self, task_id: str, stages: Iterable[PlannedStage]) -> list[StageRecord]:\\\\n        now = utcnow_iso()\\\\n        records: list[StageRecord] = []\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            for planned in stages:\\\\n                record = StageRecord(\\\\n                    stage_id=uuid.uuid4().hex,\\\\n                    task_id=task_id,\\\\n                    stage_type=planned.stage_type,\\\\n                    stage_order=planned.stage_order,\\\\n                    model_role=planned.model_role,\\\\n                    assigned_model=None,\\\\n                    assigned_provider=None,\\\\n                    status=StageStatus.PENDING,\\\\n                    handoff_doc_path=None,\\\\n                    result_summary=None,\\\\n                    token_used=0,\\\\n                    duration_sec=0.0,\\\\n                    started_at=None,\\\\n                    finished_at=None,\\\\n                    retry_count=0,\\\\n                    verify_cmd=planned.verify_cmd,\\\\n                    metadata=dict(planned.metadata),\\\\n                )\\\\n                conn.execute(\\\\n                    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                    INSERT INTO stages (\\\\n                        stage_id, task_id, stage_type, stage_order, model_role,\\\\n                        assigned_model, assigned_provider, status, handoff_doc_path,\\\\n                        result_summary, token_used, duration_sec, started_at,\\\\n                        finished_at, retry_count, verify_cmd, metadata\\\\n                    ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\\\\n                    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                    (\\\\n                        record.stage_id,\\\\n                        record.task_id,\\\\n                        record.stage_type,\\\\n                        record.stage_order,\\\\n                        record.model_role,\\\\n                        record.assigned_model,\\\\n                        record.assigned_provider,\\\\n                        record.status.value,\\\\n                        record.handoff_doc_path,\\\\n                        record.result_summary,\\\\n                        record.token_used,\\\\n                        record.duration_sec,\\\\n                        record.started_at,\\\\n                        record.finished_at,\\\\n                        record.retry_count,\\\\n                        record.verify_cmd,\\\\n                        json.dumps(record.metadata),\\\\n                    ),\\\\n                )\\\\n                records.append(record)\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"UPDATE tasks SET updated_at = ? WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (now, task_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n        return records\\\\n\\\\n    def count_stages(self, task_id: str) -> int:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\\\\"SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM stages WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\", (task_id,)).fetchone()\\\\n        return int(row[\\\\\\\"count\\\\\\\"])\\\\n\\\\n    def get_stage(self, stage_id: str) -> Optional[StageRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM stages WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\\\\", (stage_id,)).fetchone()\\\\n        return self._row_to_stage(row) if row else None\\\\n\\\\n    def list_stages(self, task_id: str) -> list[StageRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            rows = conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM stages WHERE task_id = ? ORDER BY stage_order ASC\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (task_id,),\\\\n            ).fetchall()\\\\n        return [self._row_to_stage(row) for row in rows]\\\\n\\\\n    def list_completed_stages(self, task_id: str) -> list[StageRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            rows = conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                SELECT * FROM stages\\\\n                WHERE task_id = ? AND status IN (?, ?)\\\\n                ORDER BY stage_order ASC\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (task_id, StageStatus.DONE.value, StageStatus.SKIPPED.value),\\\\n            ).fetchall()\\\\n        return [self._row_to_stage(row) for row in rows]\\\\n\\\\n    def next_pending_stage(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[StageRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                SELECT * FROM stages\\\\n                WHERE task_id = ? AND status = ?\\\\n                ORDER BY stage_order ASC\\\\n                LIMIT 1\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (task_id, StageStatus.PENDING.value),\\\\n            ).fetchone()\\\\n        return self._row_to_stage(row) if row else None\\\\n\\\\n    def assign_stage_model(self, stage_id: str, model: str, provider: str) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                UPDATE stages\\\\n                SET assigned_model = ?, assigned_provider = ?\\\\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (model, provider, stage_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def mark_stage_running(self, stage_id: str, handoff_doc_path: str) -> None:\\\\n        now = utcnow_iso()\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                UPDATE stages\\\\n                SET status = ?, handoff_doc_path = ?, started_at = ?\\\\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (StageStatus.RUNNING.value, handoff_doc_path, now, stage_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def complete_stage(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        stage_id: str,\\\\n        *,\\\\n        result_summary: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n        token_used: int = 0,\\\\n        duration_sec: float = 0.0,\\\\n    ) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                UPDATE stages\\\\n                SET status = ?, result_summary = ?, token_used = ?, duration_sec = ?,\\\\n                    finished_at = ?\\\\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (\\\\n                    StageStatus.DONE.value,\\\\n                    result_summary,\\\\n                    token_used,\\\\n                    duration_sec,\\\\n                    utcnow_iso(),\\\\n                    stage_id,\\\\n                ),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def skip_stage(self, stage_id: str, *, result_summary: Optional[str] = None) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                UPDATE stages\\\\n                SET status = ?, result_summary = ?, finished_at = ?\\\\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (StageStatus.SKIPPED.value, result_summary, utcnow_iso(), stage_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def reset_stage_to_pending(self, stage_id: str, *, retry_count: Optional[int] = None) -> None:\\\\n        query = \\\\\\\"UPDATE stages SET status = ?, started_at = NULL\\\\\\\"\\\\n        params: list[object] = [StageStatus.PENDING.value]\\\\n        if retry_count is not None:\\\\n            query += \\\\\\\", retry_count = ?\\\\\\\"\\\\n            params.append(retry_count)\\\\n        query += \\\\\\\" WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\\\\"\\\\n        params.append(stage_id)\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(query, tuple(params))\\\\n\\\\n    def fail_stage(self, stage_id: str, *, result_summary: Optional[str], retry_count: int) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                UPDATE stages\\\\n                SET status = ?, result_summary = ?, retry_count = ?, finished_at = ?\\\\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (\\\\n                    StageStatus.FAILED.value,\\\\n                    result_summary,\\\\n                    retry_count,\\\\n                    utcnow_iso(),\\\\n                    stage_id,\\\\n                ),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def log_permission_request(self, record: PermissionRequestRecord) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                INSERT INTO permission_requests (\\\\n                    request_id, stage_id, action, context, risk_level,\\\\n                    decision, voters, decided_at\\\\n                ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (\\\\n                    record.request_id,\\\\n                    record.stage_id,\\\\n                    record.action,\\\\n                    record.context,\\\\n                    record.risk_level.value,\\\\n                    record.decision,\\\\n                    json.dumps(record.voters),\\\\n                    record.decided_at,\\\\n                ),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def list_permission_requests(self, stage_id: Optional[str] = None) -> list[PermissionRequestRecord]:\\\\n        query = \\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM permission_requests\\\\\\\"\\\\n        params: tuple[object, ...] = ()\\\\n        if stage_id is not None:\\\\n            query += \\\\\\\" WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\\\\"\\\\n            params = (stage_id,)\\\\n        query += \\\\\\\" ORDER BY decided_at ASC, request_id ASC\\\\\\\"\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            rows = conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()\\\\n        records: -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,320p' scripts/orchestra_cli.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"#!/usr/bin/env python3\\\\n\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Interactive CLI for running multi-agent orchestrator workflows.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nimport argparse\\\\nimport asyncio\\\\nimport json\\\\nimport logging\\\\nimport re\\\\nimport sys\\\\nimport time\\\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\\\n\\\\nimport uuid\\\\nimport yaml\\\\n\\\\n# Fix path to allow importing from the project root if run from anywhere\\\\nsys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent.absolute()))\\\\n\\\\nfrom common.acp_client import ACPClient\\\\nfrom common.models import Message, MessagePart, RunStatus\\\\nfrom scripts.check_server_status import check_all_servers, StatusReport\\\\nfrom orchestrator.pipeline import PipelineDefinition, PipelineExecutor, PipelineError\\\\nfrom orchestrator.stage import StageDefinition, StageResult\\\\n\\\\n# Setup minimal logging, only warning+ so it doesn't clutter CLI\\\\nlogging.basicConfig(level=logging.WARNING, format=\\\\\\\"%(message)s\\\\\\\")\\\\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef hide_code_blocks(text: str) -> str:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Replaces markdown code blocks and diffs with a hidden placeholder.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    if not text:\\\\n        return \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    # Regex to match ```language \\\\\\\\n code... \\\\\\\\n ```\\\\n    pattern = re.compile(r\\\\\\\"```[a-zA-Z0-9_-]*\\\\\\\\n[\\\\\\\\s\\\\\\\\S]*?\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\", re.MULTILINE)\\\\n    return pattern.sub(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n[CODE DIFF/BLOCK HIDDEN]\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\", text)\\\\n    \\\\n\\\\nclass CLIExecutor(PipelineExecutor):\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Custom executor that streams updates to the CLI interface and routes to ACP services.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    \\\\n    def __init__(self, pipeline_def: PipelineDefinition, config_path: str):\\\\n        super().__init__(pipeline_def)\\\\n        self.session_id = uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]\\\\n        self.clients: dict[str, ACPClient] = {}\\\\n        \\\\n        with open(config_path, \\\\\\\"r\\\\\\\", encoding=\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\") as handle:\\\\n            data = yaml.safe_load(handle) or {}\\\\n            for name, info in data.get(\\\\\\\"agents\\\\\\\", {}).items():\\\\n                self.clients[name] = ACPClient(info[\\\\\\\"base_url\\\\\\\"])\\\\n                \\\\n    async def cleanup(self):\\\\n        import asyncio\\\\n        await asyncio.gather(*(client.close() for client in self.clients.values()), return_exceptions=True)\\\\n\\\\n    async def _invoke_model(self, stage_def: StageDefinition, prompt: str) -> str:\\\\n        agent_name = stage_def.metadata.get(\\\\\\\"agent\\\\\\\")\\\\n        if not agent_name:\\\\n            return await super()._invoke_model(stage_def, prompt)\\\\n            \\\\n        client = self.clients.get(agent_name)\\\\n        if not client:\\\\n            raise ValueError(f\\\\\\\"Unknown agent '{agent_name}' configured in metadata for stage {stage_def.stage_id}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        message = Message(\\\\n            role=\\\\\\\"user\\\\\\\",\\\\n            parts=[MessagePart(content=prompt)]\\\\n        )\\\\n        \\\\n        run = await client.create_run(agent_name, [message], session_id=self.session_id)\\\\n        if run.status != RunStatus.COMPLETED:\\\\n            raise RuntimeError(f\\\\\\\"Agent {agent_name} failed: {run.error}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        if not run.output_messages:\\\\n            return \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        return run.output_messages[0].text\\\\n    \\\\n    async def _execute_stage(self, stage_def: StageDefinition) -> StageResult:\\\\n        \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Override to provide CLI feedback before and after the stage.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n========================================================\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udd04 CURRENT STEP: {stage_def.name} ({stage_def.stage_id})\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\ud83e\\udd16 Agent Model:  {stage_def.model}\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"========================================================\\\\\\\")\\\\n        \\\\n        # Prepare prompt to show user what is being asked\\\\n        prompt = self._prepare_prompt(stage_def)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n[Input] -> Sending to Agent:\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n        # We don't hide code blocks in prompt for context, but we can limit size\\\\n        short_prompt = prompt if len(prompt) < 1000 else prompt[:500] + \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n...[TRUNCATED FOR DISPLAY]...\\\\\\\" + prompt[-500:]\\\\n        print(short_prompt)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u23f3 Waiting for agent response...\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\")\\\\n        \\\\n        start_time = time.time()\\\\n        try:\\\\n            output = await self._invoke_model(stage_def, prompt)\\\\n            execution_time = time.time() - start_time\\\\n            \\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"\\u2705 Agent responded in {execution_time:.1f}s\\\\\\\")\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n[Output] <- Received from Agent:\\\\\\\")\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n            \\\\n            # Hide code blocks for clean UI\\\\n            clean_output = hide_code_blocks(output)\\\\n            print(clean_output)\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n            \\\\n            return StageResult(\\\\n                stage_id=stage_def.stage_id,\\\\n                status=\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\",\\\\n                output=output,\\\\n                raw_output=output,\\\\n                execution_time=execution_time,\\\\n                model_used=stage_def.model,\\\\n            )\\\\n            \\\\n        except Exception as exc:\\\\n            execution_time = time.time() - start_time\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"\\u274c Stage failed after {execution_time:.1f}s: {exc}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            raise  # Let the base class intercept it and handle fail_fast\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nasync def ensure_servers_running(config_path: str, timeout: float = 3.0) -> bool:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Check if required servers are healthy. Return True if all ok.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udd0d Checking ACP Server Status...\\\\\\\")\\\\n    report = await check_all_servers(config_path, timeout)\\\\n    if report.unhealthy_count == 0:\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\u2705 All {report.total_servers} servers are healthy!\\\\\\\")\\\\n        return True\\\\n    \\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u274c SOME SERVERS ARE UNREACHABLE!\\\\\\\")\\\\n    for server in report.servers:\\\\n        if not server.healthy:\\\\n            err = f\\\\\\\" ({server.error})\\\\\\\" if server.error else \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"  - {server.base_url} is DOWN{err}\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nPlease start the required servers (e.g. using scripts/run_*_server.py).\\\\\\\")\\\\n    return False\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef get_available_workflows() -> list[Path]:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Return a list of workflow YAML files.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    workflows_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent / \\\\\\\"workflows\\\\\\\"\\\\n    if not workflows_dir.exists():\\\\n        return []\\\\n    return sorted(workflows_dir.glob(\\\\\\\"*.yaml\\\\\\\"))\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nasync def main_async(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\\\n    # 1. Check Servers\\\\n    servers_ok = await ensure_servers_running(args.config, args.timeout)\\\\n    if not servers_ok:\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n        \\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\" + \\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60)\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"               ORCHESTRA MULTI-AGENT CLI\\\\\\\")\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60 + \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    # 2. Select Workflow\\\\n    workflows = get_available_workflows()\\\\n    if not workflows:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\u274c No workflows found in the 'workflows/' directory.\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n        \\\\n    workflow_path = None\\\\n    if args.workflow:\\\\n        workflow_path = Path(args.workflow)\\\\n        if not workflow_path.exists():\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"\\u274c Workflow {workflow_path} not found.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            sys.exit(1)\\\\n    else:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"Available Workflows:\\\\\\\")\\\\n        for idx, wf in enumerate(workflows):\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"  [{idx + 1}] {wf.name}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        while True:\\\\n            try:\\\\n                choice = input(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nSelect a workflow (1-%d): \\\\\\\" % len(workflows))\\\\n                idx = int(choice.strip()) - 1\\\\n                if 0 <= idx < len(workflows):\\\\n                    workflow_path = workflows[idx]\\\\n                    break\\\\n                else:\\\\n                    print(\\\\\\\"Invalid choice.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            except ValueError:\\\\n                print(\\\\\\\"Please enter a number.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            except KeyboardInterrupt:\\\\n                print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nCanceled by user.\\\\\\\")\\\\n                sys.exit(0)\\\\n                \\\\n    print(f\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u2705 Selected workflow: {workflow_path.name}\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    # Load pipeline\\\\n    try:\\\\n        pipeline_def = PipelineDefinition(workflow_path)\\\\n    except PipelineError as e:\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\u274c Failed to load pipeline: {e}\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n        \\\\n    # 3. Get Task\\\\n    task_description = args.task\\\\n    if not task_description:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nEnter task description (press Enter twice to finish):\\\\\\\")\\\\n        lines = []\\\\n        while True:\\\\n            try:\\\\n                line = input()\\\\n                if not line and (not lines or not lines[-1]):\\\\n                    break\\\\n                lines.append(line)\\\\n            except KeyboardInterrupt:\\\\n                print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nCanceled by user.\\\\\\\")\\\\n                sys.exit(0)\\\\n        task_description = \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\".join(lines).strip()\\\\n        \\\\n    if not task_description:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\u274c Task description cannot be empty.\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n\\\\n    # 4. Execute Pipeline\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\ud83d\\ude80 Starting Pipeline Execution...\\\\\\\")\\\\n    executor = CLIExecutor(pipeline_def, args.config)\\\\n    \\\\n    try:\\\\n        # Also map user_task so it's available for workflows explicitly requesting it\\\\n        initial_state = {\\\\\\\"user_task\\\\\\\": task_description}\\\\n        results = await executor.execute(task_description, initial_state=initial_state)\\\\n        \\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\" + \\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\ud83c\\udf89 PIPELINE EXECUTION COMPLETED\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60)\\\\n        for stage_id, result in results.items():\\\\n            icon = \\\\\\\"\\u2705\\\\\\\" if result.status == \\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\" else \\\\\\\"\\u274c\\\\\\\"\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"{icon} Stage '{stage_id}': {result.status.upper()} ({result.execution_time:.1f}s)\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        # Check for absolute failure\\\\n        if any(r.status == \\\\\\\"failed\\\\\\\" for r in results.values()):\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u274c Pipeline completed with ERRORS.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            sys.exit(1)\\\\n            \\\\n    except Exception as e:\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u274c Pipeline execution failed due to an error: {e}\\\\\\\")\\\\n        import traceback\\\\n        traceback.print_exc()\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n    finally:\\\\n        await executor.cleanup()\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef main() -> None:\\\\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\\\\\\\"Interactive Multi-Agent Orchestrator CLI\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--config\\\\\\\", default=\\\\\\\"config/agents.yaml\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Path to agents.yaml\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--timeout\\\\\\\", type=float, default=3.0, help=\\\\\\\"Per-server health check timeout in seconds\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--workflow\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Path to a specific workflow YAML, bypasses selection\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--task\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Initial task description, bypasses input dialog\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    args = parser.parse_args()\\\\n    \\\\n    try:\\\\n        asyncio.run(main_async(args))\\\\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nExiting CLI...\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(0)\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nif __name__ == \\\\\\\"__main__\\\\\\\":\\\\n    main()\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_24\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,220p' harness/handoff.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Handoff document generation and artifact storage.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\\\nfrom typing import Iterable\\\\n\\\\nfrom harness.models import StageExecutionResult, StageRecord, TaskRecord\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass HandoffProtocol:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Persist stage inputs and outputs as markdown artifacts.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def __init__(self, artifact_root: str | Path = \\\\\\\"artifacts\\\\\\\") -> None:\\\\n        self.artifact_root = Path(artifact_root)\\\\n        self.artifact_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\\\n\\\\n    def task_dir(self, task_id: str) -> Path:\\\\n        path = self.artifact_root / task_id\\\\n        path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\\\n        return path\\\\n\\\\n    def handoff_path(self, task: TaskRecord, stage: StageRecord) -> Path:\\\\n        return self.task_dir(task.task_id) / f\\\\\\\"{stage.stage_order:02d}_{stage.stage_type}_handoff.md\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def output_path(self, task: TaskRecord, stage: StageRecord) -> Path:\\\\n        return self.task_dir(task.task_id) / f\\\\\\\"{stage.stage_order:02d}_{stage.stage_type}_output.md\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def generate(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        task: TaskRecord,\\\\n        stage: StageRecord,\\\\n        previous_stages: Iterable[StageRecord],\\\\n    ) -> str:\\\\n        previous = list(previous_stages)\\\\n        previous_stage_name = previous[-1].stage_type if previous else \\\\\\\"bootstrap\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n        lines = [\\\\n            f\\\\\\\"# Handoff: {previous_stage_name} -> {stage.stage_type}\\\\\\\",\\\\n            \\\\\\\"## Task\\\\\\\",\\\\n            task.title,\\\\n            \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n            \\\\\\\"## Description\\\\\\\",\\\\n            task.description,\\\\n            \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n        ]\\\\n\\\\n        if task.goal:\\\\n            lines.extend([\\\\\\\"## Goal\\\\\\\", task.goal, \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"])\\\\n\\\\n        lines.extend(\\\\n            [\\\\n                \\\\\\\"## Context\\\\\\\",\\\\n                f\\\\\\\"- Complexity: {task.complexity}\\\\\\\",\\\\n                f\\\\\\\"- Current Stage: {stage.stage_type} ({stage.model_role})\\\\\\\",\\\\n                f\\\\\\\"- Working Directory: {task.working_dir or '.'}\\\\\\\",\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n            ]\\\\n        )\\\\n\\\\n        if previous:\\\\n            lines.append(\\\\\\\"## Previous Stage Output\\\\\\\")\\\\n            for previous_stage in previous:\\\\n                summary = previous_stage.result_summary or \\\\\\\"No summary recorded.\\\\\\\"\\\\n                lines.extend(\\\\n                    [\\\\n                        f\\\\\\\"### {previous_stage.stage_type}\\\\\\\",\\\\n                        summary.strip(),\\\\n                        \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                    ]\\\\n                )\\\\n\\\\n        lines.append(\\\\\\\"## Current Stage Instructions\\\\\\\")\\\\n        instruction = stage.metadata.get(\\\\\\\"instructions\\\\\\\") if stage.metadata else None\\\\n        if instruction:\\\\n            lines.append(str(instruction).strip())\\\\n        else:\\\\n            lines.append(\\\\n                f\\\\\\\"Complete the `{stage.stage_type}` stage for this task in `{task.working_dir or '.'}`.\\\\\\\"\\\\n            )\\\\n        lines.append(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n        verify_cmd = stage.verify_cmd or task.verify_cmd\\\\n        if verify_cmd:\\\\n            lines.extend([\\\\\\\"## Verification\\\\\\\", f\\\\\\\"Run: `{verify_cmd}`\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"])\\\\n\\\\n        return \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\".join(lines).rstrip() + \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def write_handoff(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        task: TaskRecord,\\\\n        stage: StageRecord,\\\\n        previous_stages: Iterable[StageRecord],\\\\n    ) -> Path:\\\\n        content = self.generate(task, stage, previous_stages)\\\\n        path = self.handoff_path(task, stage)\\\\n        path.write_text(content, encoding=\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\")\\\\n        return path\\\\n\\\\n    def save_stage_output(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        task: TaskRecord,\\\\n        stage: StageRecord,\\\\n        result: StageExecutionResult,\\\\n    ) -> Path:\\\\n        path = self.output_path(task, stage)\\\\n        lines = [\\\\n            f\\\\\\\"# Output: {stage.stage_type}\\\\\\\",\\\\n            \\\\\\\"## Summary\\\\\\\",\\\\n            result.summary or \\\\\\\"No summary generated.\\\\\\\",\\\\n            \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n        ]\\\\n        if result.files_changed:\\\\n            lines.append(\\\\\\\"## Files Changed\\\\\\\")\\\\n            lines.extend(f\\\\\\\"- {path_item}\\\\\\\" for path_item in result.files_changed)\\\\n            lines.append(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\")\\\\n        if result.raw_output:\\\\n            lines.extend([\\\\\\\"## Raw Output\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"```text\\\\\\\", result.raw_output.rstrip(), \\\\\\\"```\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"])\\\\n        path.write_text(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\".join(lines).rstrip() + \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\", encoding=\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\")\\\\n        return time\\\\nfrom concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed\\\\n\\\\nfrom . import account\\\\nfrom . import quota\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef _format_bar(remaining_percent: int, width: int = 10) -> str:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Progress bar showing remaining quota (filled = remaining).\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    remaining_percent = max(0, min(100, int(remaining_percent)))\\\\n    filled = int(round(remaining_percent / 100 * width))\\\\n    filled = max(0, min(width, filled))\\\\n    return \\\\\\\"[\\\\\\\" + (\\\\\\\"\\u2588\\\\\\\" * filled) + (\\\\\\\"\\u2591\\\\\\\" * (width - filled)) + \\\\\\\"]\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef _format_reset(reset_at: int) -> str:\\\\n    if not reset_at:\\\\n        return \\\\\\\"n/a\\\\\\\"\\\\n    diff = max(0, int(reset_at) - int(time.time()))\\\\n    days = diff // 86400\\\\n    hours = (diff % 86400) // 3600\\\\n    return f\\\\\\\"{days}d {hours}h\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef _fetch_quota_for_account(acc: account.CodexAccount) -> quota.CodexQuota:\\\\n    acc = account.refresh_account_tokens(acc)\\\\n    try:\\\\n        return quota.fetch_quota(acc.access_token, acc.account_id)\\\\n    except quota.QuotaError as exc:\\\\n        if exc.status_code == 403:\\\\n            acc = account.refresh_account_tokens(acc, force=True)\\\\n            return quota.fetch_quota(acc.access_token, acc.account_id)\\\\n        raise\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef _fetch_quotas_parallel(\\\\n    accounts: list[account.CodexAccount],\\\\n) -> tuple[list[tuple[account.CodexAccount, quota.CodexQuota]], list[tuple[account.CodexAccount, Exception]]]:\\\\n    results: list[tuple[account.CodexAccount, quota.CodexQuota]] = []\\\\n    errors: list[tuple[account.CodexAccount, Exception]] = []\\\\n\\\\n    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=min(8, len(accounts))) as executor:\\\\n        futures = {executor.submit(_fetch_quota_for_account, acc): acc for acc in accounts}\\\\n        for future in as_completed(futures):\\\\n            acc = futures[future]\\\\n            try:\\\\n                results.append((acc, future.result()))\\\\n            except Exception as exc:\\\\n                errors.append((acc, exc))\\\\n\\\\n    return results, errors\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef _load_accounts_in_order() -> list[account.CodexAccount]:\\\\n    emails = account.list_accounts()\\\\n    accounts = []\\\\n    for email in emails:\\\\n        try:\\\\n            accounts.append(account.load_account(email))\\\\n        except FileNotFoundError:\\\\n            continue\\\\n    return accounts\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef cmd_status(_: argparse.Namespace) -> int:\\\\n    accounts = _load_accounts_in_order()\\\\n    if not accounts:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"No accounts found. _fetch_quotas_parallel(accounts)\\\\n\\\\n    quota_by_email: dict[str, quota.CodexQuota] = {}\\\\n    for acc, quo in results:\\\\n        quota_by_email[acc.email] = quo\\\\n        if quo.plan_type:\\\\n            acc.plan_type = quo.plan_type\\\\n        if quo.raw:\\\\n            acc.quota_snapshot = quo.raw\\\\n        account.save_account(acc)\\\\n\\\\n    errors_by_email = {acc.email: exc for acc, exc in errors}\\\\n\\\\n    max_email = max(len(acc.email) for acc in accounts)\\\\n    header = \\\\\\\"Codex Account Status\\\\\\\"\\\\n    print(header)\\\\n    print()\\\\n    print(\\\\n        f\\\\\\\"{'Account'.ljust(max_email)}  Plan   Remaining           Resets\\\\\\\"\\\\n    )\\\\n    print(\\\\n        f\\\\\\\"{'-' * max_email}  ----   -----------------  ------\\\\\\\"\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n    for acc in accounts:\\\\n        marker = \\\\\\\">\\\\\\\" if acc.email == active_email else \\\\\\\" \\\\\\\"\\\\n        if acc.email in quota_by_email:\\\\n            quo = quota_by_email[acc.email]\\\\n            remaining = 100 - quo.weekly_used_percent\\\\n            bar = _format_bar(remaining)\\\\n            pct = f\\\\\\\"{remaining:>3}%\\\\\\\"\\\\n            reset = _format_reset(quo.weekly_reset_at)\\\\n            icon = \\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udfe2\\\\\\\" if remaining > 40 else \\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udfe1\\\\\\\" if remaining > 5 else \\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udd34\\\\\\\"\\\\n            warn = \\\\\\\" \\u26a0\\\\\\\" if (quo.weekly_limit_reached or remaining <= 5) else \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n            plan = {err_msg}\\\\\\\"\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    return 0\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef cmd_list(_: argparse.Namespace) -> int:\\\\n    accounts = _load_accounts_in_order()\\\\n    active_email = -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' harness/chat2api_client.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Minimal chat2api client.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nimport json\\\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\\\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\\\\nfrom urllib import request\\\\n\\\\nimport yaml\\\\n\\\\nfrom config import settings\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass Chat2APIClient:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Simple stdlib HTTP client for the local chat2api gateway.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def __init__(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        base_url: str = settings.CHAT2API_BASE_URL,\\\\n        timeout: float = settings.CHAT2API_TIMEOUT,\\\\n        catalog_path: str | Path = settings.HARNESS_ROLE_MODELS_PATH,\\\\n    ) -> None:\\\\n        self.base_url = base_url.rstrip(\\\\\\\"/\\\\\\\")\\\\n        self.timeout = timeout\\\\n        self._model_map = self._load_model_map(catalog_path)\\\\n\\\\n    @staticmethod\\\\n    def _load_model_map(catalog_path: str | Path) -> dict[str, str]:\\\\n        \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Build semantic-name \\u2192 chat2api_id mapping from role_models.yaml.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        try:\\\\n            with open(catalog_path, \\\\\\\"r\\\\\\\", encoding=\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\") as fh:\\\\n                catalog = yaml.safe_load(fh) or {}\\\\n        except (OSError, yaml.YAMLError):\\\\n            return {}\\\\n        return {\\\\n            name: info[\\\\\\\"chat2api_id\\\\\\\"]\\\\n            for name, info in catalog.get(\\\\\\\"models\\\\\\\", {}).items()\\\\n            if \\\\\\\"chat2api_id\\\\\\\" in info\\\\n        }\\\\n\\\\n    def resolve_model(self, model: str) -> str:\\\\n        \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Map a semantic model name to its chat2api ID, passthrough if unknown.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        return self._model_map.get(model, model)\\\\n\\\\n    def list_models(self) -> list[str]:\\\\n        payload = self._get_json(\\\\\\\"/v1/models\\\\\\\")\\\\n        return [item[\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\"] for item in payload.get(\\\\\\\"data\\\\\\\", []) if \\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\" in item]\\\\n\\\\n    def chat(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        *,\\\\n        model: str,\\\\n        prompt: str,\\\\n        system: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n    ) -> str:\\\\n        messages: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\\\\n        if system:\\\\n            messages.append({\\\\\\\"role\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"system\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\": system})\\\\n        messages.append({\\\\\\\"role\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"user\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\": prompt})\\\\n        payload = self._post_json(\\\\n            \\\\\\\"/v1/chat/completions\\\\\\\",\\\\n            {\\\\n                \\\\\\\"model\\\\\\\": self.resolve_model(model),\\\\n                \\\\\\\"messages\\\\\\\": messages,\\\\n            },\\\\n        )\\\\n        return payload[\\\\\\\"choices\\\\\\\"][0][\\\\\\\"message\\\\\\\"][\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\"]\\\\n\\\\n    def _get_json(self, path: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\\\\n        req = request.Request(f\\\\\\\"{self.base_url}{path}\\\\\\\", method=\\\\\\\"GET\\\\\\\")\\\\n        with request.urlopen(req, timeout=self.timeout) as response:\\\\n            return json.loads(response.read().decode(\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\"))\\\\n\\\\n    def _post_json(self, path: str, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\\\\n        data = json.dumps(body).encode(\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\")\\\\n        req = request.Request(\\\\n            f\\\\\\\"{self.base_url}{path}\\\\\\\",\\\\n            data=data,\\\\n            headers={\\\\\\\"Content-Type\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"application/json\\\\\\\"},\\\\n            method=\\\\\\\"POST\\\\\\\",\\\\n        )\\\\n        with request.urlopen(req, timeout=self.timeout) as response:\\\\n            return slices.\\\\n\\\\nSlice 1: keep routing pure, but add an explicit Codex account activation step before Codex execution so feasibility checks do not mutate global auth state.\\\\n\\\\nSlice 2: add a nohup-style daemon manager with PID metadata, stale PID detection, hidden worker entrypoint, and SIGTERM-driven graceful shutdown around `run_forever`.\\\\n\\\\nSlice 3: add centralized logging plus JSONL stage events, then build `dashboard` aggregates from DB state and event history instead of mixing metrics into ad hoc CLI output.\\\\n\\\\nBaseline before changes: `python3 -m pytest Keep the Codex account switch separate from pure model selection, prefer a portable nohup-style daemon path for tests, and add focused tests as each slice lands so the final `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v` run is stable.\\\\n\\\\n## Verification\\\\nRun: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v`\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_48\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,220p' artifacts/563b7352a8d94c82bf26787efb827fc0/01_plan_output.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":null,\\\"status\\\":\\\"in_progress\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_48\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,220p' artifacts/563b7352a8d94c82bf26787efb827fc0/01_plan_output.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# Output: plan\\\\n## Summary\\\\nImplement the robustness work in three slices with tests after each slice:\\\\n\\\\n1. Codex account rotation\\\\n- Keep `QuotaRouter.select_model()` side-effect free so `can_run_stage()` remains safe.\\\\n- Extend routing to choose the best Codex account among all Codex quota snapshots using the existing use-it-or-lose-it scoring, and preserve the chosen `account_email` in `ModelChoice`.\\\\n- Add a separate activation step before Codex execution that calls `models.codex.account.set_active_account()` only when the chosen Codex account differs from the current active account.\\\\n- Log/surface the selected account in the routing reason and stage telemetry.\\\\n\\\\n2. Daemon lifecycle\\\\n- Add a small daemon manager module instead of embedding process control directly in CLI branches.\\\\n- Use a detached `subprocess.Popen(..., start_new_session=True)` worker for a portable nohup-style background process.\\\\n- Add `daemon start`, `daemon stop`, and `daemon status` CLI subcommands plus a hidden worker command that runs `Harness.run_forever()`.\\\\n- Persist daemon metadata in a PID file, detect stale PID files, and install a `SIGTERM` handler that lets the current iteration finish and exits cleanly.\\\\n\\\\n3. Logging, monitoring, and dashboard\\\\n- Add one centralized logging setup for the harness CLI/daemon path.\\\\n- Emit structured JSONL events for stage start, completion, retry, quota pause, permission pause, and failure.\\\\n- Compute dashboard aggregates from the DB plus the JSONL event stream: task counts by status, stage counts by status, total/average duration, total token usage, and execution error rate.\\\\n- Include daemon state, recent failures, and quota/permission pause counts in the dashboard output.\\\\n\\\\n## File Targets\\\\n- `harness/quota_router.py`\\\\n- `harness/main.py`\\\\n- `harness/stage_executor.py`\\\\n- `harness/task_queue.py`\\\\n- `config/settings.py`\\\\n- New modules are likely justified for daemon/process management and telemetry aggregation.\\\\n\\\\n## Test Plan\\\\n- Add router tests for multi-account Codex selection and no-side-effect feasibility checks.\\\\n- Add harness/main tests for Codex account activation before execution.\\\\n- Add daemon tests for PID lifecycle, stale PID detection, and stop/status behavior.\\\\n- Add telemetry/dashboard tests for JSONL event emission and aggregate summaries.\\\\n- Final verification: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v`\\\\n\\\\n## Baseline\\\\nCurrent baseline is green: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -q` passed with `21 passed`.\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_49\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"agent_message\\\",\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\u89c4\\u5212\\u7ed3\\u679c\\u5df2\\u843d\\u5230 -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' hybrid_store/hybrid_search.py\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"from __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport argparse\\nimport json\\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple\\n\\nfrom elasticsearch import Elasticsearch\\nfrom qdrant_client import QdrantClient, models\\nfrom qdrant_client.http.exceptions import UnexpectedResponse\\n\\nfrom .config import HybridConfig, load_config\\nfrom .embedder import EmbeddingClient, OllamaEmbedder\\n\\n\\ndef build_doc_filter(doc_ids: List[str]) -> Optional[models.Filter]:\\n    if not doc_ids:\\n        return None\\n    return models.Filter(\\n        must=[\\n            models.FieldCondition(\\n                key=\\\"doc_id\\\",\\n                match=models.MatchAny(any=doc_ids),\\n            )\\n        ]\\n    )\\n\\n\\ndef rrf_fuse(\\n    es_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],\\n    sparse_map: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]],\\n    dense_map: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]],\\n    k: int = 60,\\n) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\\n    es_rank = {row[\\\"doc_id\\\"]: index + 1 for index, row in enumerate(es_rows)}\\n    sparse_sorted = sorted(sparse_map.items(), key=lambda item: item[1][\\\"score\\\"], reverse=True)\\n    dense_sorted = sorted(dense_map.items(), key=lambda item: item[1][\\\"score\\\"], reverse=True)\\n    sparse_rank_map = {doc_id: index + 1 for index, (doc_id, _) in enumerate(sparse_sorted)}\\n    dense_rank_map = {doc_id: index + 1 for index, (doc_id, _) in enumerate(dense_sorted)}\\n    all_doc_ids: Set[str] = set(es_rank) | set(sparse_rank_map) | set(dense_rank_map)\\n\\n    rows: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\\n    for doc_id in all_doc_ids:\\n        score = 0.0\\n        if doc_id in es_rank:\\n            score += 1.0 / (k + es_rank[doc_id])\\n        if doc_id in sparse_rank_map:\\n            score += 1.0 / (k + sparse_rank_map[doc_id])\\n        if doc_id in dense_rank_map:\\n            score += 1.0 / (k + dense_rank_map[doc_id])\\n\\n        es_row = next((row for row in es_rows if row[\\\"doc_id\\\"] == doc_id), None)\\n        sparse_row = sparse_map.get(doc_id, {})\\n        dense_row = dense_map.get(doc_id, {})\\n        rows.append(\\n            {\\n                \\\"doc_id\\\": doc_id,\\n                \\\"hybrid_score\\\": score,\\n                \\\"title\\\": (es_row or {}).get(\\\"title\\\") or sparse_row.get(\\\"title\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                \\\"url\\\": (es_row or {}).get(\\\"url\\\") or sparse_row.get(\\\"url\\\") or dense_row.get(\\\"url\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                \\\"published_time\\\": (es_row or {}).get(\\\"published_time\\\")\\n                or sparse_row.get(\\\"published_time\\\")\\n                or dense_row.get(\\\"published_time\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                \\\"es_rank\\\": es_rank.get(doc_id),\\n                \\\"sparse_rank\\\": sparse_rank_map.get(doc_id),\\n                \\\"dense_rank\\\": dense_rank_map.get(doc_id),\\n            }\\n        )\\n    rows.sort(key=lambda item: item[\\\"hybrid_score\\\"], reverse=True)\\n    return rows\\n\\n\\ndef recommend_next_topn(hybrid_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]], candidates: List[int]) -> int:\\n    usable = [value for value in candidates if value > 0]\\n    if not usable:\\n        return 20\\n    if not hybrid_rows:\\n        return min(usable)\\n\\n    max_n = min(max(usable), len(hybrid_rows))\\n    prefix_docs: List[str] = [row[\\\"doc_id\\\"] for row in hybrid_rows[:max_n]]\\n    best_n = usable[0]\\n    prev_coverage = 0.0\\n    for candidate in sorted(set(usable)):\\n        effective_n = min(candidate, len(prefix_docs))\\n        if effective_n == 0:\\n            continue\\n        coverage = len(set(prefix_docs[:effective_n])) / float(len(set(prefix_docs[:max_n])) or 1)\\n        gain = coverage - prev_coverage\\n        best_n = candidate\\n        prev_coverage = coverage\\n        if coverage >= 0.9 and gain <= 0.08:\\n            return candidate\\n    return best_n\\n\\n\\nclass HybridSearch:\\n    def __init__(\\n        self,\\n        config: Optional[HybridConfig] = None,\\n        *,\\n        es: Optional[Elasticsearch] = None,\\n        qdrant: Optional[QdrantClient] = None,\\n        embedder: Optional[EmbeddingClient] = None,\\n    ):\\n        self.config = config or load_config()\\n        es_kwargs: Dict[str, Any] = {\\\"hosts\\\": [self.config.es_url], \\\"request_timeout\\\": 30}\\n        if self.config.es_api_key:\\n            es_kwargs[\\\"api_key\\\"] = self.config.es_api_key\\n        self.es = es or Elasticsearch(**es_kwargs)\\n        if qdrant is not None:\\n            self.qdrant = qdrant\\n        else:\\n            if not self.config.qdrant_url or not self.config.qdrant_api_key:\\n                raise RuntimeError(\\\"QDRANT_URL/QDRANT_API_KEY are required\\\")\\n            self.qdrant = QdrantClient(\\n                url=self.config.qdrant_url,\\n                api_key=self.config.qdrant_api_key,\\n                timeout=30,\\n            )\\n        self.embedder = embedder or OllamaEmbedder(\\n            self.config.ollama_url,\\n            self.config.ollama_model,\\n            self.config.ollama_timeout,\\n        )\\n\\n    def es_candidates(\\n        self,\\n        query: str,\\n        top_k: int,\\n        min_score: Optional[float] = None,\\n    ) -> Tuple[int, List[Dict[str, Any]]]:\\n        out = self.es.search(\\n            index=self.config.es_index,\\n            size=max(1, top_k),\\n            track_total_hits=True,\\n            min_score=min_score,\\n            query={\\n                \\\"bool\\\": {\\n                    \\\"should\\\": [\\n                        {\\\"match\\\": {\\\"title\\\": {\\\"query\\\": query, \\\"boost\\\": 3.0}}},\\n                        {\\\"match\\\": {\\\"full_text\\\": {\\\"query\\\": query, \\\"boost\\\": 1.0}}},\\n                    ],\\n                    \\\"minimum_should_match\\\": 1,\\n                }\\n            },\\n        )\\n        total_obj = out.get(\\\"hits\\\", {}).get(\\\"total\\\", {})\\n        total = int(total_obj.get(\\\"value\\\", 0)) if isinstance(total_obj, dict) else int(total_obj or 0)\\n        rows: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\\n        for hit in out.get(\\\"hits\\\", {}).get(\\\"hits\\\", []):\\n            src = hit.get(\\\"_source\\\", {})\\n            rows.append(\\n                {\\n                    \\\"doc_id\\\": hit.get(\\\"_id\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"es_score\\\": float(hit.get(\\\"_score\\\") or 0.0),\\n                    \\\"title\\\": src.get(\\\"title\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"url\\\": src.get(\\\"url\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"published_time\\\": src.get(\\\"published_time\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                }\\n            )\\n        return total, rows\\n\\n    def sparse_rank(\\n        self,\\n        query: str,\\n        doc_ids: List[str],\\n        top_k: int,\\n        *,\\n        use_filter: bool = True,\\n    ) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\\n        allowed_ids = set(doc_ids)\\n        query_filter = build_doc_filter(doc_ids) if use_filter else None\\n        limit = max(1, top_k)\\n        try:\\n            out = self.qdrant.query_points(\\n                collection_name=self.config.qdrant_sparse_collection,\\n                query=models.Document(text=query, model=\\\"Qdrant/bm25\\\"),\\n                using=\\\"bm25\\\",\\n                query_filter=query_filter,\\n                with_payload=True,\\n                limit=limit,\\n            )\\n        except UnexpectedResponse:\\n            out = self.qdrant.query_points(\\n                collection_name=self.config.qdrant_sparse_collection,\\n                query=models.Document(text=query, model=\\\"Qdrant/bm25\\\"),\\n                using=\\\"bm25\\\",\\n                with_payload=True,\\n                limit=max(limit * 3, 300),\\n            )\\n        points = out.points if out else []\\n        ranked: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}\\n        for point in points:\\n            payload = point.payload or {}\\n            doc_id = str(payload.get(\\\"doc_id\\\") or \\\"\\\")\\n            if not doc_id or (use_filter and allowed_ids and doc_id not in allowed_ids):\\n                continue\\n            score = float(point.score or 0.0)\\n            current = ranked.get(doc_id)\\n            if current is None or score > current[\\\"score\\\"]:\\n                ranked[doc_id] = {\\n                    \\\"score\\\": score,\\n                    \\\"title\\\": str(payload.get(\\\"title\\\") or \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"url\\\": str(payload.get(\\\"url\\\") or \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"published_time\\\": str(payload.get(\\\"published_time\\\") or \\\"\\\"),\\n                }\\n        if len(ranked) <= top_k:\\n            return ranked\\n        return dict(sorted(ranked.items(), key=lambda item: item[1][\\\"score\\\"], reverse=True)[:top_k])\\n\\n    def dense_rank(\\n        self,\\n        query_vector: List[float],\\n        doc_ids: List[str],\\n        top_k: int,\\n        *,\\n        use_filter: bool = True,\\n    ) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\\n        allowed_ids = set(doc_ids)\\n        query_filter = build_doc_filter(doc_ids) if use_filter else None\\n        limit = max(1, top_k)\\n        try:\\n            out = self.qdrant.query_points(\\n                collection_name=self.config.qdrant_dense_collection,\\n                query=query_vector,\\n                query_filter=query_filter,\\n                with_payload=True,\\n                limit=limit,\\n            )\\n        except UnexpectedResponse:\\n            out = self.qdrant.query_points(\\n                collection_name=self.config.qdrant_dense_collection,\\n                query=query_vector,\\n                with_payload=True,\\n                limit=max(limit * 3, 300),\\n            )\\n        points = out.points if out else []\\n        ranked: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}\\n        for point in points:\\n            payload = point.payload or {}\\n            doc_id = str(payload.get(\\\"doc_id\\\") or \\\"\\\")\\n            if not doc_id or (use_filter and allowed_ids and doc_id not in allowed_ids):\\n                continue\\n            score = float(point.score or 0.0)\\n            current = ranked.get(doc_id)\\n            if current is None or score > current[\\\"score\\\"]:\\n                ranked[doc_id] = {\\n                    \\\"score\\\": score,\\n                    \\\"url\\\": str(payload.get(\\\"url\\\") or \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"published_time\\\": str(payload.get(\\\"published_time\\\") or \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"text_preview\\\": str(payload.get(\\\"text_preview\\\") or \\\"\\\"),\\n                }\\n        if len(ranked) <= top_k:\\n            return ranked\\n        return dict(sorted(ranked.items(), key=lambda item: item[1][\\\"score\\\"], reverse=True)[:top_k])\\n\\n    def search(\\n        self,\\n        query: str,\\n        *,\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n./artifacts/d3dc29e5512b4ca6822fafa80ba113b6/03_e2e_test_output.md:90:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_39\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/bash -lc \\\"sed -n '260,520p' hybrid_store/hybrid_search.py\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"        *,\\n        es_top_k: int = 200,\\n        es_min_score: float = 4.5,\\n        sparse_top_k: int = 120,\\n        dense_top_k: int = 120,\\n        hybrid_top_k: int = 50,\\n        topn_candidates: Optional[List[int]] = None,\\n        sparse_scope: str = \\\"es_candidates\\\",\\n        dense_scope: str = \\\"es_candidates\\\",\\n    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n        total, es_rows = self.es_candidates(query, es_top_k, es_min_score)\\n        doc_ids = [row[\\\"doc_id\\\"] for row in es_rows if row.get(\\\"doc_id\\\")]\\n\\n        sparse_map = self.sparse_rank(\\n            query,\\n            doc_ids,\\n            sparse_top_k,\\n            use_filter=(sparse_scope == \\\"es_candidates\\\"),\\n        )\\n\\n        dense_error = \\\"\\\"\\n        dense_map: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}\\n        try:\\n            query_vector = self.embedder.embed(query)\\n            dense_map = self.dense_rank(\\n                query_vector,\\n                doc_ids,\\n                dense_top_k,\\n                use_filter=(dense_scope == \\\"es_candidates\\\"),\\n            )\\n        except Exception as exc:\\n            dense_error = str(exc)\\n\\n        fused = rrf_fuse(es_rows, sparse_map, dense_map)\\n        fused = fused[: max(1, hybrid_top_k)]\\n        recommendation = recommend_next_topn(fused, topn_candidates or [10, 20, 30, 50])\\n\\n        return {\\n            \\\"query\\\": query,\\n            \\\"collections\\\": {\\n                \\\"es_index\\\": self.config.es_index,\\n                \\\"qdrant_sparse_collection\\\": self.config.qdrant_sparse_collection,\\n                \\\"qdrant_dense_collection\\\": self.config.qdrant_dense_collection,\\n                \\\"es_index_titles\\\": self.config.es_index,\\n                \\\"qdrant_docs_collection\\\": self.config.qdrant_sparse_collection,\\n                \\\"qdrant_chunks_collection\\\": self.config.qdrant_dense_collection,\\n            },\\n            \\\"counts\\\": {\\n                \\\"es_total_hits\\\": total,\\n                \\\"es_candidates_returned\\\": len(es_rows),\\n                \\\"qdrant_sparse_ranked_docs\\\": len(sparse_map),\\n                \\\"qdrant_dense_ranked_docs\\\": len(dense_map),\\n                \\\"hybrid_ranked_docs\\\": len(fused),\\n            },\\n            \\\"scopes\\\": {\\n                \\\"sparse_scope\\\": sparse_scope,\\n                \\\"dense_scope\\\": dense_scope,\\n            },\\n            \\\"thresholds\\\": {\\n                \\\"es_min_score\\\": es_min_score,\\n            },\\n            \\\"recommended_next_top_n\\\": recommendation,\\n            \\\"ordering\\\": {\\n                \\\"top_hybrid\\\": \\\"hybrid_score_desc\\\",\\n            },\\n            \\\"dense_warning\\\": dense_error or None,\\n            \\\"hybrid_rows\\\": fused,\\n            \\\"top_hybrid\\\": fused[:10],\\n        }\\n\\n\\ndef main() -> None:\\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\\\"Hybrid search benchmark for ES + Qdrant sparse/dense/hybrid.\\\")\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\"--query\\\", required=True)\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\"--es-top-k\\\", type=int, default=200)\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\"--es-min-score\\\", type=float, default=4.5)\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\"--sparse-top-k\\\", type=int, default=120)\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\"--dense-top-k\\\", type=int, default=120)\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\"--hybrid-top-k\\\", type=int, default=50)\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\"--topn-candidates\\\", default=\\\"10,20,30,50\\\")\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\"--sparse-scope\\\", choices=[\\\"es_candidates\\\", \\\"all_docs\\\"], default=\\\"es_candidates\\\")\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\"--dense-scope\\\", choices=[\\\"es_candidates\\\", \\\"all_docs\\\"], default=\\\"es_candidates\\\")\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\"--json\\\", action=\\\"store_true\\\")\\n    args = parser.parse_args()\\n\\n    candidates = [int(value) for value in str(args.topn_candidates).split(\\\",\\\") if value.strip().isdigit()]\\n    result = HybridSearch(load_config()).search(\\n        query=args.query,\\n        es_top_k=args.es_top_k,\\n        es_min_score=args.es_min_score,\\n        sparse_top_k=args.sparse_top_k,\\n        dense_top_k=args.dense_top_k,\\n        hybrid_top_k=args.hybrid_top_k,\\n        topn_candidates=candidates or [10, 20, 30, 50],\\n        sparse_scope=args.sparse_scope,\\n        dense_scope=args.dense_scope,\\n    )\\n\\n    if args.json:\\n        print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=True, indent=2))\\n        return\\n\\n    print(f\\\"query={result['query']}\\\")\\n    print(\\n        f\\\"es_total_hits={result['counts']['es_total_hits']} \\\"\\n        f\\\"es_candidates={result['counts']['es_candidates_returned']}\\\"\\n    )\\n    print(\\n        \\\"qdrant_sparse_docs=\\\"\\n        f\\\"{result['counts']['qdrant_sparse_ranked_docs']} \\\"\\n        f\\\"qdrant_dense_docs={result['counts']['qdrant_dense_ranked_docs']}\\\"\\n    )\\n    print(\\n        f\\\"hybrid_ranked_docs={result['counts']['hybrid_ranked_docs']} \\\"\\n        f\\\"recommended_next_top_n={result['recommended_next_top_n']}\\\"\\n    )\\n    if result[\\\"dense_warning\\\"]:\\n        print(f\\\"dense_warning={result['dense_warning']}\\\")\\n    for index, row in enumerate(result[\\\"top_hybrid\\\"], 1):\\n        print(\\n            f\\\"{index:02d}. {row.get('title', '')} | {row.get('url', '')} | \\\"\\n            f\\\"hybrid={row.get('hybrid_score', 0):.6f}\\\"\\n        )\\n\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    main()\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n./artifacts/d3dc29e5512b4ca6822fafa80ba113b6/03_e2e_test_output.md:125:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_58\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/bash set(self.chat_client.list_models())\\n    55\\t        except Exception:\\n    56\\t            return self.registry.canonical_names()\\n    57\\t        if not live_ids:\\n    58\\t            return self.registry.canonical_names()\\n    59\\t        return self.registry.available_canonical_names(live_ids)\\n    60\\t\\n    61\\t    def can_run_stage(\\n    62\\t        self,\\n    63\\t    71\\t        except AllQuotaExhaustedError:\\n    72\\t            return False\\n    73\\t        return True\\n    74\\t\\n    75\\t    def select_model(\\n    76\\t        self,\\n    77\\t if snapshot is not None else None,\\n   191\\t            reason=str(parsed.get(\\\"reason\\\") or \\\"llm_selector\\\"),\\n   192\\t        )\\n   193\\t\\n   194\\t    def _fallback_select(\\n model=model,\\n   212\\t                    provider=provider,\\n   213\\t                    account_email=snapshot.email,\\n   214\\t                    reason=(\\n   215\\t                        f\\\"fallback: role={stage.model_role}, complexity={task.complexity}, \\\"\\n   216\\t                        f\\\"remaining={snapshot.remaining_percent}%, \\\"\\n   217\\t                        f\\\"reset_in={snapshot.time_until_reset_hours:.1f}h\\\"\\n   218\\t                    ),\\n   219\\t                )\\n   220\\t            else:\\n   221\\t                total_score = role_bonus + self._score_provider_without_snapshot(provider)\\n   222\\t                choice = ModelChoice(\\n   223\\t                    model=model,\\n   224\\t                    provider=provider,\\n   225\\t                    account_email=None,\\n   226\\t                    reason=f\\\"fallback: role={stage.model_role}, complexity={task.complexity}, provider={provider}\\\",\\n   227\\t 50\\t\\n    51\\tclass StageExecutor:\\n    52\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"Execute planned stages via CLI backends or lightweight API calls.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    53\\t\\n    54\\t    APPROVED_DECISIONS =   82\\t            )\\n    83\\t            duration = time.time() - started_at\\n    84\\t            return StageExecutionResult(\\n    85\\t                stage_id=stage.stage_id,\\n    86\\t def _run_github_ops(\\n   167\\t        self,\\n   168\\t        stage: StageRecord,\\n   169\\t        prompt: str,\\n   170\\t        working_dir: str,\\n   171\\t        started_at: float,\\n stderr=stderr.decode(\\\"utf-8\\\", errors=\\\"replace\\\"),\\n   243\\t        )\\n   244\\t\\n   245\\t    def _normalize_output(self, stdout: str, provider: Optional[str]) -> str:\\n   246\\t        text = stdout.strip()\\n   247\\t        if provider != \\\"codex\\\" or not text:\\n   248\\t            return text\\n   249\\t        try:\\n   250\\t            parsed = json.loads(text)\\n str):\\n   257\\t                return parsed[\\\"message\\\"]\\n   258\\t        return text\\n   259\\t\\n   260\\t    def _extract_token_usage(self, stdout: str, provider: Optional[str]) -> json.loads(text)\\n   266\\t        except json.JSONDecodeError:\\n   267\\t            return 0\\n   268\\t        return max(0, self._find_token_total(parsed))\\n   269\\t\\n   270\\t    def _summarize(self, text: str) -> str:\\n   271\\t        cleaned = \\\" \\\".join(text.split())\\n   272\\t        if len(cleaned) <= 280:\\n   273\\t            return cleaned\\n   274\\t        return cleaned[:277] + \\\"...\\\"\\n   275\\t\\n   276\\t    def _infer_provider(self, model: str) -> str:\\n   277\\t        if model.startswith(\\\"claude-\\\"):\\n   278\\t            return \\\"claude\\\"\\n   279\\t        if \\\"codex\\\" in model:\\n   280\\t            return \\\"codex\\\"\\n   281\\t        if model.startswith(\\\"gemini-\\\"):\\n   282\\t            return 286\\t\\n   287\\t    def _looks_like_quota_exhaustion(self, stdout: str, stderr: str, returncode: int = 1) -> bool:\\n   288\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Detect provider quota/rate-limit errors.\\n   289\\t\\n   290\\t        Only triggers on non-zero exit codes to avoid false positives when\\n   291\\t        the model output itself discusses quotas (e.g. planning a quota feature).\\n   292\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   293\\t        if returncode == 0:\\n   294\\t            return False\\n   295\\t        text = f\\\"{stdout}\\\\n{stderr}\\\".lower()\\n   296\\t        return any(token in text for token in (\\\"rate limit\\\", \\\"limit reached\\\", \\\"quota exceeded\\\", \\\"quota exhausted\\\", \\\"rate_limit_exceeded\\\"))\\n   297\\t\\n   298\\t    def _list_changed_files(self, working_dir: str) -> list[str]:\\n   299\\t        try:\\n   300\\t            completed = subprocess.run(\\n   301\\t                [\\\"git\\\", \\\"diff\\\", \\\"--name-only\\\"],\\n   302\\t completed.stdout.splitlines() if line.strip()]\\n   312\\t\\n   313\\t    def _safe_list_available_models(self) -> list[str] | None:\\n   314\\t        try:\\n   315\\t            return output_tokens:\\n   337\\t                    return input_tokens + output_tokens\\n   338\\t\\n   339\\t            for value in payload.values():\\n   340\\t                nested = [y/N]: \\\")\\n   562\\t    lowered = answer.strip().lower()\\n   563\\t    if lowered in {\\\"y\\\", \\\"yes\\\"}:\\n   564\\t        return True\\n   565\\t    if lowered in {\\\"n\\\", \\\"no\\\", \\\"\\\"}:\\n   566\\t        return False\\n   567\\t    return None\\n   568\\t\\n   569\\t\\n   570\\tdef build_cli_harness() -> Harness:\\n   571\\t    registry = ModelRegistry()\\n   572\\t    chat_client = Chat2APIClient()\\n   573\\t    permission_gate = PermissionGate(user_approver=_prompt_user_approval)\\n   574\\t    stage_executor = StageExecutor(chat_client=chat_client, registry=registry, permission_gate=permission_gate)\\n   575\\t    quota_router = QuotaRouter(registry=registry, chat_client=chat_client)\\n   576\\t    return Harness(quota_router=quota_router, stage_executor=stage_executor)\\n   577\\t\\n   578\\t\\n   579\\tdef build_daemon_manager() -> DaemonManager:\\n   580\\t    return DaemonManager(cwd=Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent)\\n   581\\t\\n   582\\t\\n   583\\tdef _read_multiline_description() -> str:\\n   584\\t    print(\\\"Describe the task. Press Enter twice to finish:\\\")\\n   585\\t    lines: list[str] = []\\n   586\\t    while True:\\n   587\\t        line = input()\\n   588\\t        if not line and (not lines or not lines[-1]):\\n f\\\" [{question.default}]\\\" if question.default else \\\"\\\"\\n   604\\t        response = input(f\\\"{question.prompt}{suffix}: \\\").strip()\\n   605\\t        answers[question.key] = response or (question.default or \\\"\\\")\\n   606\\t\\n   607\\t    intake = harness.intake.apply_answers(initial_description, answers)\\n   608\\t    return harness.submit_intake(intake, priority=priority, iteration.\\\")\\n   693\\t        stop_event.set()\\n   694\\t\\n   695\\t    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\\n   696\\t    for sig in (signal.SIGTERM, signal.SIGINT):\\n   697\\t        try:\\n        task_id: Optional[str] = None,\\n    44\\t    ) -> TaskRecord:\\n    45\\t        now = utcnow_iso()\\n    46\\t        record = TaskRecord(\\n    47\\t            task_id=task_id or uuid.uuid4().hex,\\n    48\\t            title=title,\\n    49\\t            description=description,\\n    50\\t            goal=goal,\\n    51\\t            verify_cmd=verify_cmd,\\n    52\\t            complexity=complexity,\\n    53\\t            priority=priority,\\n    54\\t            status=status,\\n    55\\t            working_dir=working_dir,\\n    56\\t            created_at=now,\\n updated_at\\n    65\\t                ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\\n    66\\t                \\\"\\\"\\\",\\n    67\\t                (\\n    68\\t                    record.task_id,\\n def get_task(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[TaskRecord]:\\n    84\\t        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\n    85\\t            row = conn.execute(\\\"SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE task_id = ?\\\", (task_id,)).fetchone()\\n    86\\t        return self._row_to_task(row) if row else None\\n    87\\t\\n    88\\t    def list_tasks(self, status: Optional[TaskStatus] = None) -> list[TaskRecord]:\\n ?\\\"\\n    93\\t            params = (status.value,)\\n    94\\t        query += \\\" ORDER BY priority DESC, created_at ASC\\\"\\n    95\\t        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\n    96\\t
