Optional[TaskRecord]:\\n   100\\t        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\n   101\\t            row = conn.execute(\\n   102\\t                \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   103\\t                SELECT * FROM tasks\\n   104\\t                WHERE status IN (?, ?, ?)\\n   105\\t                ORDER BY\\n   106\\t                    CASE status\\n   107\\t                        WHEN ? THEN 0\\n   108\\t                        WHEN ? THEN 1\\n   109\\t                        ELSE 2\\n   110\\t                    END,\\n   111\\t                    priority DESC,\\n   112\\t                    created_at ASC\\n   113\\t                LIMIT 1\\n   114\\t                \\\"\\\"\\\",\\n   115\\t                (\\n   116\\t                    TaskStatus.EXECUTING.value,\\n   117\\t                    TaskStatus.PENDING.value,\\n   118\\t                    TaskStatus.PLANNING.value,\\n   119\\t                    TaskStatus.EXECUTING.value,\\n   120\\t                    TaskStatus.PENDING.value,\\n   121\\t                ),\\n   122\\t            ).fetchone()\\n   123\\t        return self._row_to_task(row) if row else None\\n   124\\t\\n   125\\t    def update_task_status(self, task_id: str, status: TaskStatus) -> None:\\n   126\\t        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\n   127\\t            conn.execute(\\n   128\\t                \\\"UPDATE tasks SET status = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE task_id = ?\\\",\\n   129\\t                (status.value, utcnow_iso(), task_id),\\n   130\\t            )\\n   131\\t\\n   132\\t    def update_task_working_dir(self, task_id: str, working_dir: str) -> None:\\n   133\\t        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\n   134\\t            conn.execute(\\n   135\\t                \\\"UPDATE tasks SET working_dir = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE task_id = ?\\\",\\n   136\\t                (working_dir, utcnow_iso(), task_id),\\n   137\\t            )\\n   138\\t\\n   139\\t    def save_stages(self, task_id: str, stages: Iterable[PlannedStage]) -> list[StageRecord]:\\n   140\\t        now = utcnow_iso()\\n   141\\t        records: list[StageRecord] = []\\n   142\\t        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\n   143\\t    assigned_model=None,\\n   151\\t                    assigned_provider=None,\\n   152\\t                    status=StageStatus.PENDING,\\n   153\\t                    handoff_doc_path=None,\\n   154\\t                    result_summary=None,\\n   155\\t                    token_used=0,\\n   156\\t                    duration_sec=0.0,\\n   157\\t                    started_at=None,\\n   158\\t metadata=dict(planned.metadata),\\n   162\\t                )\\n   163\\t                conn.execute(\\n   164\\t                    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   165\\t                    INSERT INTO stages (\\n   166\\t                        stage_id, task_id, stage_type, stage_order, model_role,\\n   167\\t                        assigned_model, assigned_provider, status, handoff_doc_path,\\n   168\\t (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\\n   171\\t                    \\\"\\\"\\\",\\n   172\\t                    (\\n   173\\t                        record.stage_id,\\n   174\\t                        record.task_id,\\n   175\\t                        record.stage_type,\\n   176\\t                        record.stage_order,\\n   177\\t                        record.model_role,\\n 193\\t            conn.execute(\\n   194\\t                \\\"UPDATE tasks SET updated_at = ? WHERE task_id = ?\\\",\\n   195\\t                (now, task_id),\\n   196\\t            )\\n   197\\t        return records\\n   198\\t\\n   199\\t    def count_stages(self, task_id: str) -> int:\\n   200\\t        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\n   201\\t            row = conn.execute(\\\"SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM stages WHERE task_id = ?\\\", (task_id,)).fetchone()\\n   202\\t        return int(row[\\\"count\\\"])\\n   203\\t\\n   204\\t    def get_stage(self, stage_id: str) -> Optional[StageRecord]:\\n   205\\t        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\n   206\\t            row = conn.execute(\\\"SELECT * FROM stages WHERE stage_id = ?\\\", (stage_id,)).fetchone()\\n   207\\t        return self._row_to_stage(row) if row else None\\n   208\\t\\n   209\\t    def list_stages(self, task_id: str) -> list[StageRecord]:\\n   210\\t        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\n   211\\t            rows = conn.execute(\\n   212\\t                \\\"SELECT * FROM stages WHERE task_id = ? AND status = ?\\n   235\\t                ORDER BY stage_order ASC\\n   236\\t                LIMIT 1\\n   237\\t                \\\"\\\"\\\",\\n   238\\t                (task_id, StageStatus.PENDING.value),\\n   239\\t            ).fetchone()\\n   240\\t        return self._row_to_stage(row) if row else None\\n   241\\t\\n   242\\t    def assign_stage_model(\\n   243\\t        self,\\n   244\\t        stage_id: str,\\n   245\\t        model: str,\\n   246\\t        provider: str,\\n   247\\t        *,\\n   248\\t        account_email: Optional[str] = None,\\n   249\\t        reason: Optional[str] = None,\\n   250\\t    ) -> None:\\n   251\\t        metadata = self._load_stage_metadata(stage_id)\\n   252\\t        metadata[\\\"selected_account_email\\\"] = account_email\\n   253\\t        if reason:\\n   254\\t            metadata[\\\"selection_reason\\\"] = reason\\n   255\\t        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\n   256\\t            conn.execute(\\n   257\\t                \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   258\\t                UPDATE stages\\n   259\\t                SET assigned_model = ?, assigned_provider = ?, metadata = ?\\n   260\\t                WHERE stage_id = ?\\n   261\\t                \\\"\\\"\\\",\\n   262\\t                (model, provider, json.dumps(metadata), stage_id),\\n   263\\t            )\\n   264\\t\\n   265\\t    def update_stage_metadata(self, stage_id: str, metadata_patch: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\n   266\\t        metadata = self._load_stage_metadata(stage_id)\\n   267\\t        metadata.update(metadata_patch)\\n   268\\t        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\n   269\\t            conn.execute(\\n   270\\t                \\\"UPDATE stages SET metadata = ? WHERE stage_id = ?\\\",\\n   271\\t                (json.dumps(metadata), stage_id),\\n   272\\t            )\\n   273\\t\\n   274\\t    def mark_stage_running(self, stage_id: str, handoff_doc_path: -lc \\\"nl -ba harness/model_registry.py | sed -n '1,320p'\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"     1\\t\\\"\\\"\\\"Unified model name resolution.\\n     2\\t\\n     3\\tEvery model has one canonical name (the key in role_models.yaml).\\n     4\\tEach model may declare aliases — alternative names used by chat2api,\\n     5\\tCLI tools, or shorthand references.\\n     6\\t\\n     7\\tModelRegistry enforces a strict invariant: **no alias may point to\\n     8\\tmore than one canonical name**.  This is validated at load time so\\n     9\\tthat name collisions are caught early rather than at runtime.\\n    10\\t\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    11\\tfrom __future__ import annotations\\n    12\\t\\n    13\\tfrom pathlib import Path\\n    14\\tfrom typing import Any\\n    15\\t\\n    16\\timport yaml\\n    17\\t\\n    18\\tfrom config import settings\\n    19\\t\\n    20\\t\\n    21\\tclass AliasCollisionError(ValueError):\\n    22\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"Raised when an alias resolves to more than one canonical model.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    23\\t\\n    24\\t\\n    25\\tclass ModelRegistry:\\n    26\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"Single source of truth for model name resolution.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    27\\t\\n    28\\t    def __init__(self, catalog_path: str | Path = settings.HARNESS_ROLE_MODELS_PATH) -> None:\\n    29\\t        self._catalog = self._load(catalog_path)\\n    30\\t        self._models: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = self._catalog.get(\\\"models\\\", {})\\n    31\\t        # canonical name → set of aliases (including the canonical name itself)\\n    32\\t        self._alias_to_canonical: dict[str, str] = {}\\n    33\\t        self._build_index()\\n    34\\t\\n    35\\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n    36\\t    # Public API\\n    37\\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n    38\\t\\n    39\\t    def resolve(self, name: str) -> str:\\n    40\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Resolve any name (canonical or alias) to its canonical name.\\n    41\\t\\n    42\\t        Returns the input unchanged if it is not recognised — this keeps\\n    43\\t        the system open to ad-hoc model names that haven't been catalogued.\\n    44\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    45\\t        return self._alias_to_canonical.get(name, name)\\n    46\\t\\n    47\\t    def chat2api_id(self, name: str) -> str:\\n    48\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the chat2api model ID for *name* (canonical or alias).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    49\\t        canonical = self.resolve(name)\\n    50\\t        info = self._models.get(canonical, {})\\n    51\\t        return info.get(\\\"chat2api_id\\\", canonical)\\n    52\\t\\n    53\\t    def cli_model_id(self, name: str) -> str:\\n    54\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the CLI model ID for *name* (canonical or alias).\\n    55\\t\\n    56\\t        Falls back to the canonical name if no cli_model_id is configured.\\n    57\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    58\\t        canonical = self.resolve(name)\\n    59\\t        info = self._models.get(canonical, {})\\n    60\\t        return info.get(\\\"cli_model_id\\\", canonical)\\n    61\\t\\n    62\\t    def provider(self, name: str) -> str:\\n    63\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the provider string for *name*.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    64\\t        canonical = self.resolve(name)\\n    65\\t        return self._models.get(canonical, {}).get(\\\"provider\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n    66\\t\\n    67\\t    def get_info(self, name: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\\n    68\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the full catalog entry for *name*.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    69\\t        canonical = self.resolve(name)\\n    70\\t        return dict(self._models.get(canonical, {}))\\n    71\\t\\n    72\\t    def canonical_names(self) -> list[str]:\\n    73\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Return all canonical model names.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    74\\t        return list(self._models.keys())\\n    75\\t\\n    76\\t    def available_canonical_names(self, live_ids: set[str]) -> list[str]:\\n    77\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Return canonical names whose chat2api_id appears in *live_ids*.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    78\\t        return [\\n    79\\t            name for name in self._models\\n    80\\t            if self.chat2api_id(name) in live_ids\\n    81\\t        ]\\n    82\\t\\n    83\\t    def roles(self) -> dict[str, list[str]]:\\n    84\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Return role → [canonical model names] mapping.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    85\\t        return dict(self._catalog.get(\\\"roles\\\", {}))\\n    86\\t\\n    87\\t    def models_for_role(self, role: str) -> list[str]:\\n    88\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Return ordered candidate list for *role*.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    89\\t        roles = self._catalog.get(\\\"roles\\\", {})\\n    90\\t        configured = roles.get(role)\\n    91\\t        if configured:\\n    92\\t            return list(configured)\\n    93\\t        return self.canonical_names()\\n    94\\t\\n    95\\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n    96\\t    # Internals\\n    97\\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n    98\\t\\n    99\\t    @staticmethod\\n   100\\t    def _load(path: str | Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\\n   101\\t        with open(path, \\\"r\\\", encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\") as fh:\\n   102\\t            return yaml.safe_load(fh) or {}\\n   103\\t\\n   104\\t    def _build_index(self) -> None:\\n   105\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Build alias → canonical mapping with collision detection.\\n   106\\t\\n   107\\t        Only the canonical name and explicit aliases are registered.\\n   108\\t        chat2api_id and cli_model_id are *not* registered as aliases\\n   109\\t        because multiple models may share a gateway ID (e.g. sonnet\\n   110\\t        and haiku both use ``copilot-claude``).  Outbound resolution\\n   111\\t        uses the dedicated ``chat2api_id()`` / ``cli_model_id()``\\n   112\\t        methods which look up the value directly.\\n   113\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   114\\t        for canonical, info in self._models.items():\\n   115\\t            self._register(canonical, canonical)\\n   116\\t            for alias in info.get(\\\"aliases\\\", []):\\n   117\\t                if alias and alias != canonical:\\n   118\\t                    self._register(alias, canonical)\\n   119\\t\\n   120\\t    def _register(self, alias: str, canonical: str) -> None:\\n   121\\t        existing = self._alias_to_canonical.get(alias)\\n   122\\t        if existing is not None and existing != canonical:\\n   123\\t            raise AliasCollisionError(\\n   124\\t                f\\\"Alias {alias!r} maps to both {existing!r} and {canonical!r}. \\\"\\n   125\\t                f\\\"Each alias must resolve to exactly one canonical model.\\\"\\n   126\\t            )\\n   127\\t        self._alias_to_canonical[alias] = canonical\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n./artifacts/d3dc29e5512b4ca6822fafa80ba113b6/01_plan_output.md:31:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_12\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/bash -lc \\\"sed -n '1,320p' hybrid_store/hybrid_search.py\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"from __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport argparse\\nimport json\\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple\\n\\nfrom elasticsearch import Elasticsearch\\nfrom qdrant_client import QdrantClient, models\\nfrom qdrant_client.http.exceptions import UnexpectedResponse\\n\\nfrom .config import HybridConfig, load_config\\nfrom .embedder import EmbeddingClient, OllamaEmbedder\\n\\n\\ndef build_doc_filter(doc_ids: List[str]) -> Optional[models.Filter]:\\n    if not doc_ids:\\n        return None\\n    return models.Filter(\\n        must=[\\n            models.FieldCondition(\\n                key=\\\"doc_id\\\",\\n                match=models.MatchAny(any=doc_ids),\\n            )\\n        ]\\n    )\\n\\n\\ndef rrf_fuse(\\n    es_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]],\\n    sparse_map: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]],\\n    dense_map: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]],\\n    k: int = 60,\\n) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\\n    es_rank = {row[\\\"doc_id\\\"]: index + 1 for index, row in enumerate(es_rows)}\\n    sparse_sorted = sorted(sparse_map.items(), key=lambda item: item[1][\\\"score\\\"], reverse=True)\\n    dense_sorted = sorted(dense_map.items(), key=lambda item: item[1][\\\"score\\\"], reverse=True)\\n    sparse_rank_map = {doc_id: index + 1 for index, (doc_id, _) in enumerate(sparse_sorted)}\\n    dense_rank_map = {doc_id: index + 1 for index, (doc_id, _) in enumerate(dense_sorted)}\\n    all_doc_ids: Set[str] = set(es_rank) | set(sparse_rank_map) | set(dense_rank_map)\\n\\n    rows: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\\n    for doc_id in all_doc_ids:\\n        score = 0.0\\n        if doc_id in es_rank:\\n            score += 1.0 / (k + es_rank[doc_id])\\n        if doc_id in sparse_rank_map:\\n            score += 1.0 / (k + sparse_rank_map[doc_id])\\n        if doc_id in dense_rank_map:\\n            score += 1.0 / (k + dense_rank_map[doc_id])\\n\\n        es_row = next((row for row in es_rows if row[\\\"doc_id\\\"] == doc_id), None)\\n        sparse_row = sparse_map.get(doc_id, {})\\n        dense_row = dense_map.get(doc_id, {})\\n        rows.append(\\n            {\\n                \\\"doc_id\\\": doc_id,\\n                \\\"hybrid_score\\\": score,\\n                \\\"title\\\": (es_row or {}).get(\\\"title\\\") or sparse_row.get(\\\"title\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                \\\"url\\\": (es_row or {}).get(\\\"url\\\") or sparse_row.get(\\\"url\\\") or dense_row.get(\\\"url\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                \\\"published_time\\\": (es_row or {}).get(\\\"published_time\\\")\\n                or sparse_row.get(\\\"published_time\\\")\\n                or dense_row.get(\\\"published_time\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                \\\"es_rank\\\": es_rank.get(doc_id),\\n                \\\"sparse_rank\\\": sparse_rank_map.get(doc_id),\\n                \\\"dense_rank\\\": dense_rank_map.get(doc_id),\\n            }\\n        )\\n    rows.sort(key=lambda item: item[\\\"hybrid_score\\\"], reverse=True)\\n    return rows\\n\\n\\ndef recommend_next_topn(hybrid_rows: List[Dict[str, Any]], candidates: List[int]) -> int:\\n    usable = [value for value in candidates if value > 0]\\n    if not usable:\\n        return 20\\n    if not hybrid_rows:\\n        return min(usable)\\n\\n    max_n = min(max(usable), len(hybrid_rows))\\n    prefix_docs: List[str] = [row[\\\"doc_id\\\"] for row in hybrid_rows[:max_n]]\\n    best_n = usable[0]\\n    prev_coverage = 0.0\\n    for candidate in sorted(set(usable)):\\n        effective_n = min(candidate, len(prefix_docs))\\n        if effective_n == 0:\\n            continue\\n        coverage = len(set(prefix_docs[:effective_n])) / float(len(set(prefix_docs[:max_n])) or 1)\\n        gain = coverage - prev_coverage\\n        best_n = candidate\\n        prev_coverage = coverage\\n        if coverage >= 0.9 and gain <= 0.08:\\n            return candidate\\n    return best_n\\n\\n\\nclass HybridSearch:\\n    def __init__(\\n        self,\\n        config: Optional[HybridConfig] = None,\\n        *,\\n        es: Optional[Elasticsearch] = None,\\n        qdrant: Optional[QdrantClient] = None,\\n        embedder: Optional[EmbeddingClient] = None,\\n    ):\\n        self.config = config or load_config()\\n        es_kwargs: Dict[str, Any] = {\\\"hosts\\\": [self.config.es_url], \\\"request_timeout\\\": 30}\\n        if self.config.es_api_key:\\n            es_kwargs[\\\"api_key\\\"] = self.config.es_api_key\\n        self.es = es or Elasticsearch(**es_kwargs)\\n        if qdrant is not None:\\n            self.qdrant = qdrant\\n        else:\\n            if not self.config.qdrant_url or not self.config.qdrant_api_key:\\n                raise RuntimeError(\\\"QDRANT_URL/QDRANT_API_KEY are required\\\")\\n            self.qdrant = QdrantClient(\\n                url=self.config.qdrant_url,\\n                api_key=self.config.qdrant_api_key,\\n                timeout=30,\\n            )\\n        self.embedder = embedder or OllamaEmbedder(\\n            self.config.ollama_url,\\n            self.config.ollama_model,\\n            self.config.ollama_timeout,\\n        )\\n\\n    def es_candidates(\\n        self,\\n        query: str,\\n        top_k: int,\\n        min_score: Optional[float] = None,\\n    ) -> Tuple[int, List[Dict[str, Any]]]:\\n        out = self.es.search(\\n            index=self.config.es_index,\\n            size=max(1, top_k),\\n            track_total_hits=True,\\n            min_score=min_score,\\n            query={\\n                \\\"bool\\\": {\\n                    \\\"should\\\": [\\n                        {\\\"match\\\": {\\\"title\\\": {\\\"query\\\": query, \\\"boost\\\": 3.0}}},\\n                        {\\\"match\\\": {\\\"full_text\\\": {\\\"query\\\": query, \\\"boost\\\": 1.0}}},\\n                    ],\\n                    \\\"minimum_should_match\\\": 1,\\n                }\\n            },\\n        )\\n        total_obj = out.get(\\\"hits\\\", {}).get(\\\"total\\\", {})\\n        total = int(total_obj.get(\\\"value\\\", 0)) if isinstance(total_obj, dict) else int(total_obj or 0)\\n        rows: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\\n        for hit in out.get(\\\"hits\\\", {}).get(\\\"hits\\\", []):\\n            src = hit.get(\\\"_source\\\", {})\\n            rows.append(\\n                {\\n                    \\\"doc_id\\\": hit.get(\\\"_id\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"es_score\\\": float(hit.get(\\\"_score\\\") or 0.0),\\n                    \\\"title\\\": src.get(\\\"title\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"url\\\": src.get(\\\"url\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"published_time\\\": src.get(\\\"published_time\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                }\\n            )\\n        return total, rows\\n\\n    def sparse_rank(\\n        self,\\n        query: str,\\n        doc_ids: List[str],\\n        top_k: int,\\n        *,\\n        use_filter: bool = True,\\n    ) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\\n        allowed_ids = set(doc_ids)\\n        query_filter = build_doc_filter(doc_ids) if use_filter else None\\n        limit = max(1, top_k)\\n        try:\\n            out = self.qdrant.query_points(\\n                collection_name=self.config.qdrant_sparse_collection,\\n                query=models.Document(text=query, model=\\\"Qdrant/bm25\\\"),\\n                using=\\\"bm25\\\",\\n                query_filter=query_filter,\\n                with_payload=True,\\n                limit=limit,\\n            )\\n        except UnexpectedResponse:\\n            out = self.qdrant.query_points(\\n                collection_name=self.config.qdrant_sparse_collection,\\n                query=models.Document(text=query, model=\\\"Qdrant/bm25\\\"),\\n                using=\\\"bm25\\\",\\n                with_payload=True,\\n                limit=max(limit * 3, 300),\\n            )\\n        points = out.points if out else []\\n        ranked: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}\\n        for point in points:\\n            payload = point.payload or {}\\n            doc_id = str(payload.get(\\\"doc_id\\\") or \\\"\\\")\\n            if not doc_id or (use_filter and allowed_ids and doc_id not in allowed_ids):\\n                continue\\n            score = float(point.score or 0.0)\\n            current = ranked.get(doc_id)\\n            if current is None or score > current[\\\"score\\\"]:\\n                ranked[doc_id] = {\\n                    \\\"score\\\": score,\\n                    \\\"title\\\": str(payload.get(\\\"title\\\") or \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"url\\\": str(payload.get(\\\"url\\\") or \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"published_time\\\": str(payload.get(\\\"published_time\\\") or \\\"\\\"),\\n                }\\n        if len(ranked) <= top_k:\\n            return ranked\\n        return dict(sorted(ranked.items(), key=lambda item: item[1][\\\"score\\\"], reverse=True)[:top_k])\\n\\n    def dense_rank(\\n        self,\\n        query_vector: List[float],\\n        doc_ids: List[str],\\n        top_k: int,\\n        *,\\n        use_filter: bool = True,\\n    ) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\\n        allowed_ids = set(doc_ids)\\n        query_filter = build_doc_filter(doc_ids) if use_filter else None\\n        limit = max(1, top_k)\\n        try:\\n            out = self.qdrant.query_points(\\n                collection_name=self.config.qdrant_dense_collection,\\n                query=query_vector,\\n                query_filter=query_filter,\\n                with_payload=True,\\n                limit=limit,\\n            )\\n        except UnexpectedResponse:\\n            out = self.qdrant.query_points(\\n                collection_name=self.config.qdrant_dense_collection,\\n                query=query_vector,\\n                with_payload=True,\\n                limit=max(limit * 3, 300),\\n            )\\n        points = out.points if out else []\\n        ranked: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}\\n        for point in points:\\n            payload = point.payload or {}\\n            doc_id = str(payload.get(\\\"doc_id\\\") or \\\"\\\")\\n            if not doc_id or (use_filter and allowed_ids and doc_id not in allowed_ids):\\n                continue\\n            score = float(point.score or 0.0)\\n            current = ranked.get(doc_id)\\n            if current is None or score > current[\\\"score\\\"]:\\n                ranked[doc_id] = {\\n                    \\\"score\\\": score,\\n                    \\\"url\\\": str(payload.get(\\\"url\\\") or \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"published_time\\\": str(payload.get(\\\"published_time\\\") or \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"text_preview\\\": str(payload.get(\\\"text_preview\\\") or \\\"\\\"),\\n                }\\n        if len(ranked) <= top_k:\\n            return ranked\\n        return dict(sorted(ranked.items(), key=lambda item: item[1][\\\"score\\\"], reverse=True)[:top_k])\\n\\n    def search(\\n        self,\\n        query: str,\\n        *,\\n        es_top_k: int = 200,\\n        es_min_score: float = 4.5,\\n        sparse_top_k: int = 120,\\n        dense_top_k: int = 120,\\n        hybrid_top_k: int = 50,\\n        topn_candidates: Optional[List[int]] = None,\\n        sparse_scope: str = \\\"es_candidates\\\",\\n        dense_scope: str = \\\"es_candidates\\\",\\n    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n        total, es_rows = self.es_candidates(query, es_top_k, es_min_score)\\n        doc_ids = [row[\\\"doc_id\\\"] for row in es_rows if row.get(\\\"doc_id\\\")]\\n\\n        sparse_map = self.sparse_rank(\\n            query,\\n            doc_ids,\\n            sparse_top_k,\\n            use_filter=(sparse_scope == \\\"es_candidates\\\"),\\n        )\\n\\n        dense_error = \\\"\\\"\\n        dense_map: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}\\n        try:\\n            query_vector = self.embedder.embed(query)\\n            dense_map = self.dense_rank(\\n                query_vector,\\n                doc_ids,\\n                dense_top_k,\\n                use_filter=(dense_scope == \\\"es_candidates\\\"),\\n            )\\n        except Exception as exc:\\n            dense_error = str(exc)\\n\\n        fused = rrf_fuse(es_rows, sparse_map, dense_map)\\n        fused = fused[: max(1, hybrid_top_k)]\\n        recommendation = recommend_next_topn(fused, topn_candidates or [10, 20, 30, 50])\\n\\n        return {\\n            \\\"query\\\": query,\\n            \\\"collections\\\": {\\n                \\\"es_index\\\": self.config.es_index,\\n                \\\"qdrant_sparse_collection\\\": self.config.qdrant_sparse_collection,\\n                \\\"qdrant_dense_collection\\\": self.config.qdrant_dense_collection,\\n                \\\"es_index_titles\\\": self.config.es_index,\\n                \\\"qdrant_docs_collection\\\": self.config.qdrant_sparse_collection,\\n                \\\"qdrant_chunks_collection\\\": self.config.qdrant_dense_collection,\\n            },\\n            \\\"counts\\\": {\\n                \\\"es_total_hits\\\": total,\\n                \\\"es_candidates_returned\\\": len(es_rows),\\n                \\\"qdrant_sparse_ranked_docs\\\": len(sparse_map),\\n                \\\"qdrant_dense_ranked_docs\\\": len(dense_map),\\n                \\\"hybrid_ranked_docs\\\": len(fused),\\n            },\\n            \\\"scopes\\\": {\\n                \\\"sparse_scope\\\": sparse_scope,\\n                \\\"dense_scope\\\": dense_scope,\\n            },\\n            \\\"thresholds\\\": {\\n                \\\"es_min_score\\\": es_min_score,\\n            },\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n./artifacts/d3dc29e5512b4ca6822fafa80ba113b6/01_plan_output.md:33:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_13\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/bash -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' hybrid_store/reader.py\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"#!/usr/bin/env python3\\nimport argparse\\nimport fcntl\\nimport json\\nimport os\\nimport re\\nimport sys\\nimport uuid\\nfrom contextlib import contextmanager\\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, Iterator, Optional\\n\\nfrom elasticsearch import Elasticsearch\\n\\nfrom .config import HybridConfig, load_config as load_hybrid_config\\n\\n\\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\\nclass ReaderConfig:\\n    es_url: str\\n    es_api_key: str\\n    es_index_titles: str\\n    workspace_dir: Path\\n    state_dir: Path\\n\\n\\ndef load_config() -> ReaderConfig:\\n    hybrid_cfg: HybridConfig = load_hybrid_config()\\n    workspace_dir = Path(\\n        os.environ.get(\\\"SWIMSWAM_READER_WORKSPACE\\\")\\n        or os.environ.get(\\\"MEMORY_WORKSPACE\\\")\\n        or os.path.expanduser(\\\"~/.openclaw/workspace\\\")\\n    )\\n    state_dir = Path(\\n        os.environ.get(\\\"SWIMSWAM_READER_STATE_DIR\\\")\\n        or (workspace_dir / \\\".reader_state\\\" / \\\"swimswam\\\")\\n    )\\n    return ReaderConfig(\\n        es_url=hybrid_cfg.es_url,\\n        es_api_key=hybrid_cfg.es_api_key,\\n        es_index_titles=hybrid_cfg.es_index,\\n        workspace_dir=workspace_dir,\\n        state_dir=state_dir,\\n    )\\n\\n\\ndef connect_es(cfg: ReaderConfig) -> Elasticsearch:\\n    kwargs: Dict[str, Any] = {\\\"hosts\\\": [cfg.es_url], \\\"request_timeout\\\": 30}\\n    if cfg.es_api_key:\\n        kwargs[\\\"api_key\\\"] = cfg.es_api_key\\n    return Elasticsearch(**kwargs)\\n\\n\\ndef utc_now_iso() -> str:\\n    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()\\n\\n\\ndef sanitize_stream_id(stream_id: str) -> str:\\n    value = re.sub(r\\\"[^A-Za-z0-9._-]+\\\", \\\"_\\\", (stream_id or \\\"\\\").strip())\\n    return value[:120].strip(\\\"._-\\\") or \\\"default\\\"\\n\\n\\nclass ReaderStateStore:\\n    def __init__(self, state_dir: Path):\\n        self.state_dir = state_dir\\n        self.state_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n\\n    def path_for(self, stream_id: str) -> Path:\\n        return self.state_dir / f\\\"{sanitize_stream_id(stream_id)}.json\\\"\\n\\n    @contextmanager\\n    def locked_state(self, stream_id: str) -> Iterator[tuple[Path, Any, Dict[str, Any]]]:\\n        path = self.path_for(stream_id)\\n        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n        with open(path, \\\"a+\\\", encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\") as handle:\\n            fcntl.flock(handle.fileno(), fcntl.LOCK_EX)\\n            try:\\n                handle.seek(0)\\n                raw = handle.read()\\n                state: Dict[str, Any]\\n                if raw.strip():\\n                    try:\\n                        state = json.loads(raw)\\n                    except json.JSONDecodeError:\\n                        state = {}\\n                else:\\n                    state = {}\\n                yield path, handle, state\\n            finally:\\n                fcntl.flock(handle.fileno(), fcntl.LOCK_UN)\\n\\n    @staticmethod\\n    def write_locked(handle: Any, state: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\n        handle.seek(0)\\n        handle.truncate(0)\\n        json.dump(state, handle, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2, sort_keys=True)\\n        handle.write(\\\"\\\\n\\\")\\n        handle.flush()\\n\\n\\nORDER_OPTIONS = {\\n    \\\"published_time_asc\\\": {\\\"field\\\": \\\"published_time\\\", \\\"direction\\\": \\\"asc\\\"},\\n    \\\"published_time_desc\\\": {\\\"field\\\": \\\"published_time\\\", \\\"direction\\\": \\\"desc\\\"},\\n    \\\"homepage_time_desc\\\": {\\\"field\\\": \\\"homepage_time\\\", \\\"direction\\\": \\\"desc\\\"},\\n}\\nTIEBREAKER_FIELD = \\\"url\\\"\\n\\nDECISION_COUNTERS = {\\n    \\\"useful\\\": \\\"useful\\\",\\n    \\\"skip\\\": \\\"skipped\\\",\\n    \\\"error\\\": \\\"errors\\\",\\n}\\n\\n\\nclass SwimSwamReader:\\n    def __init__(\\n        self,\\n        es: Elasticsearch,\\n        cfg: ReaderConfig,\\n        state_store: Optional[ReaderStateStore] = None,\\n    ):\\n        self.es = es\\n        self.cfg = cfg\\n        self.state_store = state_store or ReaderStateStore(cfg.state_dir)\\n\\n    def status(self, stream_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n        with self.state_store.locked_state(stream_id) as (_path, _handle, state):\\n            return self._status_payload(stream_id, state, include_article=True)\\n\\n    def reset(self, stream_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n        with self.state_store.locked_state(stream_id) as (path, handle, _state):\\n            handle.seek(0)\\n            handle.truncate(0)\\n            handle.flush()\\n            try:\\n                path.unlink(missing_ok=True)\\n            except OSError:\\n                pass\\n        return {\\n            \\\"stream_id\\\": stream_id,\\n            \\\"reset\\\": True,\\n            \\\"state_path\\\": str(self.state_store.path_for(stream_id)),\\n        }\\n\\n    def next_article(\\n        self,\\n        stream_id: str,\\n        order_by: str,\\n        max_chars: int,\\n    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n        if order_by not in ORDER_OPTIONS:\\n            raise ValueError(f\\\"unsupported order_by: {order_by}\\\")\\n        max_chars = max(500, int(max_chars))\\n\\n        with self.state_store.locked_state(stream_id) as (_path, handle, state):\\n            self._initialize_state_if_needed(state, stream_id, order_by)\\n            if state.get(\\\"order_by\\\") != order_by:\\n                raise ValueError(\\n                    f\\\"stream '{stream_id}' already uses order_by={state.get('order_by')}; reset it before changing order\\\"\\n                )\\n\\n            inflight = state.get(\\\"inflight\\\")\\n            if inflight:\\n                hit = self._get_hit_by_id(str(inflight.get(\\\"doc_id\\\") or \\\"\\\"))\\n                if hit is not None:\\n                    payload = self._article_payload(\\n                        hit,\\n                        state=state,\\n                        stream_id=stream_id,\\n                        lease_id=str(inflight.get(\\\"lease_id\\\") or \\\"\\\"),\\n                        max_chars=max_chars,\\n                        resumed=True,\\n                    )\\n                    payload[\\\"state_path\\\"] = str(self.state_store.path_for(stream_id))\\n                    return payload\\n                state[\\\"inflight\\\"] = None\\n                state[\\\"updated_at\\\"] = utc_now_iso()\\n                self.state_store.write_locked(handle, state)\\n\\n            hit = self._search_next_hit(state)\\n            if hit is None:\\n                payload = self._status_payload(stream_id, state, include_article=False)\\n                payload.update(\\n                    {\\n                        \\\"done\\\": True,\\n                        \\\"message\\\": \\\"no_more_articles\\\",\\n                        \\\"state_path\\\": str(self.state_store.path_for(stream_id)),\\n                    }\\n                )\\n                return payload\\n\\n            lease_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\\n            state[\\\"inflight\\\"] = {\\n                \\\"lease_id\\\": lease_id,\\n                \\\"doc_id\\\": hit[\\\"_id\\\"],\\n                \\\"leased_at\\\": utc_now_iso(),\\n                \\\"sort\\\": hit.get(\\\"sort\\\") or [],\\n            }\\n            state[\\\"updated_at\\\"] = utc_now_iso()\\n            self.state_store.write_locked(handle, state)\\n            payload = self._article_payload(\\n                hit,\\n                state=state,\\n                stream_id=stream_id,\\n                lease_id=lease_id,\\n                max_chars=max_chars,\\n                resumed=False,\\n            )\\n            payload[\\\"state_path\\\"] = str(self.state_store.path_for(stream_id))\\n            return payload\\n\\n    def commit(self, stream_id: str, lease_id: str, decision: str, note: str = \\\"\\\") -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n        decision_key = (decision or \\\"\\\").strip().lower()\\n        if decision_key not in DECISION_COUNTERS:\\n            raise ValueError(f\\\"unsupported decision: {decision}\\\")\\n\\n        with self.state_store.locked_state(stream_id) as (_path, handle, state):\\n            inflight = state.get(\\\"inflight\\\")\\n            if not inflight:\\n                return {\\n                    \\\"stream_id\\\": stream_id,\\n                    \\\"committed\\\": False,\\n                    \\\"error\\\": \\\"no_inflight_article\\\",\\n                }\\n            if str(inflight.get(\\\"lease_id\\\") or \\\"\\\") != lease_id:\\n                return {\\n                    \\\"stream_id\\\": stream_id,\\n                    \\\"committed\\\": False,\\n                    \\\"error\\\": \\\"lease_mismatch\\\",\\n                    \\\"expected_lease_id\\\": inflight.get(\\\"lease_id\\\"),\\n                }\\n\\n            state[\\\"cursor\\\"] = {\\n                \\\"doc_id\\\": inflight.get(\\\"doc_id\\\"),\\n                \\\"sort\\\": inflight.get(\\\"sort\\\") or [],\\n                \\\"committed_at\\\": utc_now_iso(),\\n                \\\"decision\\\": decision_key,\\n            }\\n            stats = state.setdefault(\\\"stats\\\", {})\\n            stats[\\\"committed\\\"] = int(stats.get(\\\"committed\\\", 0)) + 1\\n            counter_key = DECISION_COUNTERS[decision_key]\\n            stats[counter_key] = int(stats.get(counter_key, 0)) + 1\\n            if note.strip():\\n                state[\\\"last_note\\\"] = note.strip()\\n            state[\\\"last_committed_doc_id\\\"] = inflight.get(\\\"doc_id\\\")\\n            state[\\\"inflight\\\"] = None\\n            state[\\\"updated_at\\\"] = utc_now_iso()\\n            self.state_store.write_locked(handle, state)\\n\\n            return {\\n                \\\"stream_id\\\": stream_id,\\n                \\\"committed\\\": True,\\n                \\\"decision\\\": decision_key,\\n                \\\"doc_id\\\": state[\\\"cursor\\\"].get(\\\"doc_id\\\"),\\n                \\\"cursor\\\": state[\\\"cursor\\\"],\\n                \\\"stats\\\": state.get(\\\"stats\\\", {}),\\n                \\\"state_path\\\": str(self.state_store.path_for(stream_id)),\\n            }\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n./artifacts/d3dc29e5512b4ca6822fafa80ba113b6/01_plan_output.md:39:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_16\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/bash -lc \\\"sed -n '260,520p' hybrid_store/reader.py\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"            }\\n\\n    def _initialize_state_if_needed(self, state: Dict[str, Any], stream_id: str, order_by: str) -> None:\\n        if state.get(\\\"stream_id\\\"):\\n            return\\n\\n        order_cfg = ORDER_OPTIONS[order_by]\\n        snapshot_hit = self.es.search(\\n            index=self.cfg.es_index_titles,\\n            size=1,\\n            sort=[\\n                {order_cfg[\\\"field\\\"]: {\\\"order\\\": \\\"desc\\\", \\\"missing\\\": \\\"_last\\\"}},\\n                {TIEBREAKER_FIELD: {\\\"order\\\": \\\"desc\\\", \\\"missing\\\": \\\"_last\\\"}},\\n            ],\\n            query=self._base_query(order_cfg[\\\"field\\\"]),\\n            _source=False,\\n        )\\n        hits = (snapshot_hit.get(\\\"hits\\\") or {}).get(\\\"hits\\\") or []\\n        snapshot_sort_value = hits[0].get(\\\"sort\\\", [None])[0] if hits else None\\n        state.update(\\n            {\\n                \\\"stream_id\\\": stream_id,\\n                \\\"order_by\\\": order_by,\\n                \\\"created_at\\\": utc_now_iso(),\\n                \\\"updated_at\\\": utc_now_iso(),\\n                \\\"snapshot_upper_bound\\\": snapshot_sort_value,\\n                \\\"cursor\\\": None,\\n                \\\"inflight\\\": None,\\n                \\\"stats\\\": {\\n                    \\\"committed\\\": 0,\\n                    \\\"useful\\\": 0,\\n                    \\\"skipped\\\": 0,\\n                    \\\"errors\\\": 0,\\n                },\\n            }\\n        )\\n\\n    def _search_next_hit(self, state: Dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\\n        order_cfg = ORDER_OPTIONS[state[\\\"order_by\\\"]]\\n        query = self._base_query(order_cfg[\\\"field\\\"])\\n        snapshot_upper_bound = state.get(\\\"snapshot_upper_bound\\\")\\n        if snapshot_upper_bound is not None:\\n            query[\\\"bool\\\"][\\\"filter\\\"].append(\\n                {\\\"range\\\": {order_cfg[\\\"field\\\"]: {\\\"lte\\\": snapshot_upper_bound}}}\\n            )\\n\\n        body: Dict[str, Any] = {\\n            \\\"index\\\": self.cfg.es_index_titles,\\n            \\\"size\\\": 1,\\n            \\\"sort\\\": [\\n                {order_cfg[\\\"field\\\"]: {\\\"order\\\": order_cfg[\\\"direction\\\"], \\\"missing\\\": \\\"_last\\\"}},\\n                {TIEBREAKER_FIELD: {\\\"order\\\": order_cfg[\\\"direction\\\"], \\\"missing\\\": \\\"_last\\\"}},\\n            ],\\n            \\\"query\\\": query,\\n            \\\"_source\\\": [\\n                \\\"url\\\",\\n                \\\"title\\\",\\n                \\\"published_time\\\",\\n                \\\"homepage_time\\\",\\n                \\\"updated_at\\\",\\n                \\\"fetch_status\\\",\\n                \\\"comment_count_hint\\\",\\n                \\\"full_text\\\",\\n            ],\\n        }\\n        cursor = state.get(\\\"cursor\\\") or {}\\n        if cursor.get(\\\"sort\\\"):\\n            body[\\\"search_after\\\"] = cursor[\\\"sort\\\"]\\n        out = self.es.search(**body)\\n        hits = (out.get(\\\"hits\\\") or {}).get(\\\"hits\\\") or []\\n        return hits[0] if hits else None\\n\\n    def _get_hit_by_id(self, doc_id: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\\n        if not doc_id:\\n            return None\\n        try:\\n            out = self.es.get(index=self.cfg.es_index_titles, id=doc_id)\\n        except Exception:\\n            return None\\n        source = out.get(\\\"_source\\\") or {}\\n        return {\\\"_id\\\": out.get(\\\"_id\\\"), \\\"_source\\\": source}\\n\\n    @staticmethod\\n    def _base_query(sort_field: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n        return {\\n            \\\"bool\\\": {\\n                \\\"filter\\\": [\\n                    {\\\"term\\\": {\\\"fetch_status\\\": \\\"success\\\"}},\\n                    {\\\"exists\\\": {\\\"field\\\": sort_field}},\\n                    {\\\"exists\\\": {\\\"field\\\": \\\"full_text\\\"}},\\n                    {\\\"exists\\\": {\\\"field\\\": TIEBREAKER_FIELD}},\\n                ]\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n    def _status_payload(\\n        self,\\n        stream_id: str,\\n        state: Dict[str, Any],\\n        include_article: bool,\\n    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n        inflight = state.get(\\\"inflight\\\") or {}\\n        payload = {\\n            \\\"stream_id\\\": stream_id,\\n            \\\"exists\\\": bool(state.get(\\\"stream_id\\\")),\\n            \\\"order_by\\\": state.get(\\\"order_by\\\"),\\n            \\\"snapshot_upper_bound\\\": state.get(\\\"snapshot_upper_bound\\\"),\\n            \\\"cursor\\\": state.get(\\\"cursor\\\"),\\n            \\\"stats\\\": state.get(\\\"stats\\\", {}),\\n            \\\"inflight\\\": inflight or None,\\n            \\\"state_path\\\": str(self.state_store.path_for(stream_id)),\\n        }\\n        if include_article and inflight.get(\\\"doc_id\\\"):\\n            hit = self._get_hit_by_id(str(inflight[\\\"doc_id\\\"]))\\n            if hit is not None:\\n                payload[\\\"inflight_article\\\"] = {\\n                    \\\"doc_id\\\": hit.get(\\\"_id\\\"),\\n                    \\\"title\\\": (hit.get(\\\"_source\\\") or {}).get(\\\"title\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"url\\\": (hit.get(\\\"_source\\\") or {}).get(\\\"url\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n                    \\\"published_time\\\": (hit.get(\\\"_source\\\") or {}).get(\\\"published_time\\\"),\\n                    \\\"homepage_time\\\": (hit.get(\\\"_source\\\") or {}).get(\\\"homepage_time\\\"),\\n                }\\n        return payload\\n\\n    @staticmethod\\n    def _article_payload(\\n        hit: Dict[str, Any],\\n        *,\\n        state: Dict[str, Any],\\n        stream_id: str,\\n        lease_id: str,\\n        max_chars: int,\\n        resumed: bool,\\n    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n        source = hit.get(\\\"_source\\\") or {}\\n        full_text = str(source.get(\\\"full_text\\\") or \\\"\\\")\\n        visible_text = full_text[:max_chars]\\n        inflight = state.get(\\\"inflight\\\") or {}\\n        return {\\n            \\\"stream_id\\\": stream_id,\\n            \\\"order_by\\\": state.get(\\\"order_by\\\"),\\n            \\\"lease_id\\\": lease_id,\\n            \\\"doc_id\\\": hit.get(\\\"_id\\\"),\\n            \\\"title\\\": source.get(\\\"title\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n            \\\"url\\\": source.get(\\\"url\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n            \\\"published_time\\\": source.get(\\\"published_time\\\"),\\n            \\\"homepage_time\\\": source.get(\\\"homepage_time\\\"),\\n            \\\"updated_at\\\": source.get(\\\"updated_at\\\"),\\n            \\\"comment_count_hint\\\": int(source.get(\\\"comment_count_hint\\\") or 0),\\n            \\\"full_text\\\": visible_text,\\n            \\\"full_text_chars\\\": len(full_text),\\n            \\\"returned_chars\\\": len(visible_text),\\n            \\\"truncated\\\": len(visible_text) < len(full_text),\\n            \\\"cursor_preview\\\": {\\n                \\\"snapshot_upper_bound\\\": state.get(\\\"snapshot_upper_bound\\\"),\\n                \\\"current_sort\\\": inflight.get(\\\"sort\\\") if resumed else hit.get(\\\"sort\\\"),\\n                \\\"last_committed\\\": state.get(\\\"cursor\\\"),\\n            },\\n            \\\"resumed_inflight\\\": resumed,\\n        }\\n\\n\\ndef print_json(payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\n    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)\\n    sys.stdout.write(\\\"\\\\n\\\")\\n\\n\\ndef main() -> None:\\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\\\"Sequential SwimSwam reader for OpenClaw\\\")\\n    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest=\\\"command\\\", required=True)\\n\\n    p_next = sub.add_parser(\\\"next\\\", help=\\\"Lease the next article without advancing the committed cursor\\\")\\n    p_next.add_argument(\\\"--stream-id\\\", required=True)\\n    p_next.add_argument(\\\"--order-by\\\", default=\\\"published_time_desc\\\", choices=sorted(ORDER_OPTIONS))\\n    p_next.add_argument(\\\"--max-chars\\\", type=int, default=12000)\\n    p_next.add_argument(\\\"--json\\\", action=\\\"store_true\\\", dest=\\\"as_json\\\")\\n\\n    p_commit = sub.add_parser(\\\"commit\\\", help=\\\"Commit the currently leased article and advance the cursor\\\")\\n    p_commit.add_argument(\\\"--stream-id\\\", required=True)\\n    p_commit.add_argument(\\\"--lease-id\\\", required=True)\\n    p_commit.add_argument(\\\"--decision\\\", required=True, choices=sorted(DECISION_COUNTERS))\\n    p_commit.add_argument(\\\"--note\\\", default=\\\"\\\")\\n    p_commit.add_argument(\\\"--json\\\", action=\\\"store_true\\\", dest=\\\"as_json\\\")\\n\\n    p_status = sub.add_parser(\\\"status\\\", help=\\\"Inspect reader state\\\")\\n    p_status.add_argument(\\\"--stream-id\\\", required=True)\\n    p_status.add_argument(\\\"--json\\\", action=\\\"store_true\\\", dest=\\\"as_json\\\")\\n\\n    p_reset = sub.add_parser(\\\"reset\\\", help=\\\"Reset reader state\\\")\\n    p_reset.add_argument(\\\"--stream-id\\\", required=True)\\n    p_reset.add_argument(\\\"--json\\\", action=\\\"store_true\\\", dest=\\\"as_json\\\")\\n\\n    args = parser.parse_args()\\n    cfg = load_config()\\n    reader = SwimSwamReader(connect_es(cfg), cfg)\\n\\n    if args.command == \\\"next\\\":\\n        result = reader.next_article(args.stream_id, args.order_by, args.max_chars)\\n    elif args.command == \\\"commit\\\":\\n        result = reader.commit(args.stream_id, args.lease_id, args.decision, args.note)\\n    elif args.command == \\\"status\\\":\\n        result = reader.status(args.stream_id)\\n    elif args.command == \\\"reset\\\":\\n        result = reader.reset(args.stream_id)\\n    else:\\n        raise ValueError(f\\\"unknown command: {args.command}\\\")\\n\\n    if getattr(args, \\\"as_json\\\", False):\\n        print_json(result)\\n    else:\\n        print_json(result)\\n\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n trying to update status.\\\")\\n        return\\n    store.update_fetch_status(record[\\\"url\\\"], status, new_comment_count=new_comment_count)\\n\\n\\ndef batch_mode_memory_enabled(pending_count: mode == \\\"headed+proxy\\\":\\n        os.environ[\\\"SWIMSWAM_BROWSER_MODE\\\"] = \\\"headed\\\"\\n        os.environ[\\\"SWIMSWAM_PROXY_MODE\\\"] = \\\"force\\\"\\n    elif mode == \\\"headless+proxy\\\":\\n        os.environ[\\\"SWIMSWAM_BROWSER_MODE\\\"] = \\\"headless\\\"\\n        os.environ[\\\"SWIMSWAM_PROXY_MODE\\\"] = \\\"force\\\"\\n    elif mode == \\\"headed\\\":\\n Enum):\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    PENDING = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"pending\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    PLANNING = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"planning\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    EXECUTING = StageStatus(str, Enum):\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    PENDING = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"pending\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    RUNNING = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"running\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    DONE = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"done\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    FAILED = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skipped\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nclass RiskLevel(str, Enum):\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    SAFE = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"safe\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    MODERATE = False\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.started\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_16\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/bin/bash harness/db.py\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":null,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"in_progress\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_16\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"sed -n '1,360p' harness/db.py\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"SQLite schema and connection helpers for the harness.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nimport sqlite3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nfrom contextlib import contextmanager\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nfrom typing import Iterator\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nSCHEMA = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nPRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    task_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    title TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    description TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    goal TEXT,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    verify_cmd TEXT,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    complexity TEXT DEFAULT 'medium',\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    priority INTEGER DEFAULT 50,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    working_dir TEXT,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n);\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stages (\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    stage_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    task_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(task_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    stage_type TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    stage_order INTEGER NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    model_role TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    assigned_model TEXT,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    assigned_provider TEXT,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    handoff_doc_path TEXT,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    result_summary TEXT,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    token_used INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    duration_sec REAL DEFAULT 0,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    started_at TEXT,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    finished_at TEXT,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    verify_cmd TEXT,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    metadata TEXT DEFAULT '{}'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n);\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS permission_requests (\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    request_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    stage_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES stages(stage_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    action TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    context TEXT,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    risk_level TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    decision TEXT,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    voters TEXT DEFAULT '[]',\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    decided_at TEXT\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n);\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS quota_events (\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    event_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    provider TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    account_email TEXT,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    details TEXT DEFAULT '{}',\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    created_at TEXT NOT NULL\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n);\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_status_priority\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    ON tasks(status, priority DESC, created_at ASC);\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stages_task_order\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    ON stages(task_id, stage_order ASC);\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stages_task_status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    ON stages(task_id, status, stage_order ASC);\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nclass HarnessDB:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Small wrapper around a SQLite database file.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    def __init__(self, db_path: str | Path) -> None:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        self.db_path = Path(db_path)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        self.db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        self.initialize()\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    def initialize(self) -> None:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        with self.connect() as conn:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            conn.executescript(SCHEMA)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    @contextmanager\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    def connect(self) -> Iterator[sqlite3.Connection]:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        conn.execute(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        try:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            yield conn\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            conn.commit()\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        except Exception:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            conn.rollback()\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            raise\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        finally:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            conn.close()\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.started\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_17\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"sed -n '1,360p' harness/task_queue.py\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":null,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"in_progress\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_17\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"sed -n '1,360p' harness/task_queue.py\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"SQLite-backed task and stage queue.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nimport json\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nimport uuid\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nfrom typing import Iterable, Optional\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nfrom harness.db import HarnessDB\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nfrom harness.models import (\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    PermissionRequestRecord,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    PlannedStage,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    QuotaEventRecord,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    RiskLevel,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    StageRecord,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    StageStatus,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    TaskRecord,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    TaskStatus,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ndef utcnow_iso() -> str:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nclass TaskQueue:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"CRUD layer for tasks, stages, and audit tables.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    def __init__(self, db: HarnessDB) -> None:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        self.db = db\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    def create_task(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        self,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        title: str,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        description: str,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        *,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        goal: Optional[str] = None,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        verify_cmd: Optional[str] = None,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        complexity: str = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"medium\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        priority: int = 50,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.PENDING,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        working_dir: Optional[str] = None,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        task_id: Optional[str] = None,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    ) -> TaskRecord:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        now = utcnow_iso()\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        record = THEN 1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                        ELSE 2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    END,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    priority DESC,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    created_at ASC\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                LIMIT 1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                (\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    TaskStatus.EXECUTING.value,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    TaskStatus.PENDING.value,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    TaskStatus.PLANNING.value,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    TaskStatus.EXECUTING.value,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    TaskStatus.PENDING.value,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                ),\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            ).fetchone()\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        return self._row_to_task(row) if row else None\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    def update_task_status(self, task_id: str, status: TaskStatus) -> None:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"UPDATE tasks SET status = ?, updated_at = ? Context\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Complexity: complex\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Current Stage: code (coder)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Working Directory: /home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Previous Stage Output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n### plan\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nImplement in three slices.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nSlice 1: keep routing pure, but add an explicit Codex account activation step before Codex execution so feasibility checks do not mutate global auth state.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nSlice 2: add a nohup-style daemon manager with PID metadata, stale PID detection, hidden worker entrypoint, and SIGTERM-driven graceful shutdown around `run_forever`.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nSlice 3: add centralized logging plus JSONL stage events, then build `dashboard` aggregates from DB state and event history instead of mixing metrics into ad hoc CLI output.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nBaseline before changes: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -q` => `21 passed`.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Current Stage Instructions\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nImplement all three features end-to-end. Keep the Codex account switch separate from pure model selection, prefer a portable nohup-style daemon path for tests, and add focused tests as each slice lands so the final `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v` run is stable.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Verification\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nRun: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ artifacts/563b7352a8d94c82bf26787efb827fc0/01_plan_output.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"# Output: plan\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Summary\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nImplement the robustness work in three slices with tests after each slice:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n1. Codex account rotation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Keep `QuotaRouter.select_model()` side-effect free so `can_run_stage()` remains safe.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Extend routing to choose the best Codex account among all Codex quota snapshots using the existing use-it-or-lose-it scoring, and preserve the chosen `account_email` in `ModelChoice`.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add a separate activation step before Codex execution that calls `models.codex.account.set_active_account()` only when the chosen Codex account differs from the current active account.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Log/surface the selected account in the routing reason and stage telemetry.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n2. Daemon lifecycle\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add a small daemon manager module instead of embedding process control directly in CLI branches.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Use a detached `subprocess.Popen(..., start_new_session=True)` worker for a portable nohup-style background process.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add `daemon start`, `daemon stop`, and `daemon status` CLI subcommands plus a hidden worker command that runs `Harness.run_forever()`.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Persist daemon metadata in a PID file, detect stale PID files, and install a `SIGTERM` handler that lets the current iteration finish and exits cleanly.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n3. Logging, monitoring, and dashboard\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add one centralized logging setup for the harness CLI/daemon path.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Emit structured JSONL events for stage start, completion, retry, quota pause, permission pause, and failure.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Compute dashboard aggregates from the DB plus the JSONL event stream: task counts by status, stage counts by status, total/average duration, total token usage, and execution error rate.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Include daemon state, recent failures, and quota/permission pause counts in the dashboard output.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## File Targets\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- `harness/quota_router.py`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- `harness/main.py`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- `harness/stage_executor.py`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- `harness/task_queue.py`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- `config/settings.py`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- New modules are likely justified for daemon/process management and telemetry aggregation.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Test Plan\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add router tests for multi-account Codex selection and no-side-effect feasibility checks.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add harness/main tests for Codex account activation before execution.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add daemon tests for PID lifecycle, stale PID detection, and stop/status behavior.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add telemetry/dashboard tests for JSONL event emission and aggregate summaries.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Final verification: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Baseline\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nCurrent baseline is green: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -q` passed with `21
