{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_16","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' tests/harness/test_task_queue.py\"","aggregated_output":"from harness.db import HarnessDB\nfrom harness.models import PlannedStage, TaskStatus\nfrom harness.task_queue import TaskQueue\n\n\ndef test_task_queue_creates_and_orders_tasks(tmp_path) -> None:\n    queue = TaskQueue(HarnessDB(tmp_path / \"harness.db\"))\n\n    low = queue.create_task(\"low\", \"low priority task\", priority=10, status=TaskStatus.PENDING)\n    high = queue.create_task(\"high\", \"high priority task\", priority=90, status=TaskStatus.EXECUTING)\n    queue.save_stages(high.task_id, [PlannedStage(stage_type=\"code\", stage_order=1, model_role=\"coder\")])\n    queue.save_stages(low.task_id, [PlannedStage(stage_type=\"code\", stage_order=1, model_role=\"coder\")])\n\n    picked = queue.pick_next_runnable_task()\n\n    assert picked is not None\n    assert picked.task_id == high.task_id\n    assert queue.next_pending_stage(high.task_id).stage_type == \"code\"\n\n\ndef test_task_queue_updates_stage_lifecycle(tmp_path) -> None:\n    queue = TaskQueue(HarnessDB(tmp_path / \"harness.db\"))\n    task = queue.create_task(\"task\", \"desc\", status=TaskStatus.EXECUTING)\n    [stage] = queue.save_stages(\n        task.task_id,\n        [\n            PlannedStage(\n                stage_type=\"code\",\n                stage_order=1,\n                model_role=\"coder\",\n                verify_cmd=\"pytest tests/test_feature.py\",\n            )\n        ],\n    )\n\n    queue.assign_stage_model(\n        stage.stage_id,\n        \"gpt-5.4-codex\",\n        \"codex\",\n        account_email=\"codex-2@test\",\n        reason=\"use-it-or-lose-it\",\n    )\n    queue.mark_stage_running(stage.stage_id, \"/tmp/handoff.md\")\n    queue.complete_stage(stage.stage_id, result_summary=\"Implemented feature.\", token_used=123, duration_sec=4.5)\n\n    loaded = queue.get_stage(stage.stage_id)\n    assert loaded is not None\n    assert loaded.assigned_model == \"gpt-5.4-codex\"\n    assert loaded.handoff_doc_path == \"/tmp/handoff.md\"\n    assert loaded.result_summary == \"Implemented feature.\"\n    assert loaded.token_used == 123\n    assert loaded.duration_sec == 4.5\n    assert loaded.metadata[\"selected_account_email\"] == \"codex-2@test\"\n    assert loaded.metadata[\"selection_reason\"] == \"use-it-or-lose-it\"\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_17","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '260,520p' harness/task_queue.py\"","aggregated_output":"                WHERE stage_id = ?\n                \"\"\",\n                (model, provider, json.dumps(metadata), stage_id),\n            )\n\n    def update_stage_metadata(self, stage_id: str, metadata_patch: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        metadata = self._load_stage_metadata(stage_id)\n        metadata.update(metadata_patch)\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            conn.execute(\n                \"UPDATE stages SET metadata = ? WHERE stage_id = ?\",\n                (json.dumps(metadata), stage_id),\n            )\n\n    def mark_stage_running(self, stage_id: str, handoff_doc_path: str) -> None:\n        now = utcnow_iso()\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            conn.execute(\n                \"\"\"\n                UPDATE stages\n                SET status = ?, handoff_doc_path = ?, started_at = ?\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\n                \"\"\",\n                (StageStatus.RUNNING.value, handoff_doc_path, now, stage_id),\n            )\n\n    def complete_stage(\n        self,\n        stage_id: str,\n        *,\n        result_summary: Optional[str] = None,\n        token_used: int = 0,\n        duration_sec: float = 0.0,\n    ) -> None:\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            conn.execute(\n                \"\"\"\n                UPDATE stages\n                SET status = ?, result_summary = ?, token_used = ?, duration_sec = ?,\n                    finished_at = ?\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\n                \"\"\",\n                (\n                    StageStatus.DONE.value,\n                    result_summary,\n                    token_used,\n                    duration_sec,\n                    utcnow_iso(),\n                    stage_id,\n                ),\n            )\n\n    def skip_stage(self, stage_id: str, *, result_summary: Optional[str] = None) -> None:\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            conn.execute(\n                \"\"\"\n                UPDATE stages\n                SET status = ?, result_summary = ?, finished_at = ?\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\n                \"\"\",\n                (StageStatus.SKIPPED.value, result_summary, utcnow_iso(), stage_id),\n            )\n\n    def reset_stage_to_pending(\n        self, stage_id: str, *, retry_count: Optional[int] = None, clear_model: bool = True\n    ) -> None:\n        query = \"UPDATE stages SET status = ?, started_at = NULL\"\n        params: list[object] = [StageStatus.PENDING.value]\n        if clear_model:\n            query += \", assigned_model = NULL, assigned_provider = NULL\"\n        if retry_count is not None:\n            query += \", retry_count = ?\"\n            params.append(retry_count)\n        query += \" WHERE stage_id = ?\"\n        params.append(stage_id)\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            conn.execute(query, tuple(params))\n\n    def fail_stage(self, stage_id: str, *, result_summary: Optional[str], retry_count: int) -> None:\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            conn.execute(\n                \"\"\"\n                UPDATE stages\n                SET status = ?, result_summary = ?, retry_count = ?, finished_at = ?\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\n                \"\"\",\n                (\n                    StageStatus.FAILED.value,\n                    result_summary,\n                    retry_count,\n                    utcnow_iso(),\n                    stage_id,\n                ),\n            )\n\n    def log_permission_request(self, record: PermissionRequestRecord) -> None:\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            conn.execute(\n                \"\"\"\n                INSERT INTO permission_requests (\n                    request_id, stage_id, action, context, risk_level,\n                    decision, voters, decided_at\n                ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n                \"\"\",\n                (\n                    record.request_id,\n                    record.stage_id,\n                    record.action,\n                    record.context,\n                    record.risk_level.value,\n                    record.decision,\n                    json.dumps(record.voters),\n                    record.decided_at,\n                ),\n            )\n\n    def list_permission_requests(self, stage_id: Optional[str] = None) -> list[PermissionRequestRecord]:\n        query = \"SELECT * FROM permission_requests\"\n        params: tuple[object, ...] = ()\n        if stage_id is not None:\n            query += \" WHERE stage_id = ?\"\n            params = (stage_id,)\n        query += \" ORDER BY decided_at ASC, request_id ASC\"\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            rows = conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()\n        records: list[PermissionRequestRecord] = []\n        for row in rows:\n            records.append(\n                PermissionRequestRecord(\n                    request_id=row[\"request_id\"],\n                    stage_id=row[\"stage_id\"],\n                    action=row[\"action\"],\n                    context=row[\"context\"],\n                    risk_level=RiskLevel(row[\"risk_level\"]),\n                    decision=row[\"decision\"],\n                    voters=json.loads(row[\"voters\"] or \"[]\"),\n                    decided_at=row[\"decided_at\"],\n                )\n            )\n        return records\n\n    def log_quota_event(self, record: QuotaEventRecord) -> None:\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            conn.execute(\n                \"\"\"\n                INSERT INTO quota_events (\n                    event_id, provider, account_email, event_type, details, created_at\n                ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n                \"\"\",\n                (\n                    record.event_id,\n                    record.provider,\n                    record.account_email,\n                    record.event_type,\n                    json.dumps(record.details),\n                    record.created_at,\n                ),\n            )\n\n    def list_quota_events(self, provider: Optional[str] = None) -> list[QuotaEventRecord]:\n        query = \"SELECT * FROM quota_events\"\n        params: tuple[object, ...] = ()\n        if provider is not None:\n            query += \" WHERE provider = ?\"\n            params = (provider,)\n        query += \" ORDER BY created_at ASC, event_id ASC\"\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            rows = conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()\n        records: list[QuotaEventRecord] = []\n        for row in rows:\n            records.append(\n                QuotaEventRecord(\n                    event_id=row[\"event_id\"],\n                    provider=row[\"provider\"],\n                    account_email=row[\"account_email\"],\n                    event_type=row[\"event_type\"],\n                    details=json.loads(row[\"details\"] or \"{}\"),\n                    created_at=row[\"created_at\"],\n                )\n            )\n        return records\n\n    def _row_to_task(self, row) -> TaskRecord:\n        return TaskRecord(\n            task_id=row[\"task_id\"],\n            title=row[\"title\"],\n            description=row[\"description\"],\n            goal=row[\"goal\"],\n            verify_cmd=row[\"verify_cmd\"],\n            complexity=row[\"complexity\"],\n            priority=row[\"priority\"],\n            status=TaskStatus(row[\"status\"]),\n            working_dir=row[\"working_dir\"],\n            created_at=row[\"created_at\"],\n            updated_at=row[\"updated_at\"],\n        )\n\n    def _row_to_stage(self, row) -> StageRecord:\n        metadata = row[\"metadata\"]\n        parsed_metadata = json.loads(metadata) if metadata else {}\n        return StageRecord(\n            stage_id=row[\"stage_id\"],\n            task_id=row[\"task_id\"],\n            stage_type=row[\"stage_type\"],\n            stage_order=row[\"stage_order\"],\n            model_role=row[\"model_role\"],\n            assigned_model=row[\"assigned_model\"],\n            assigned_provider=row[\"assigned_provider\"],\n            status=StageStatus(row[\"status\"]),\n            handoff_doc_path=row[\"handoff_doc_path\"],\n            result_summary=row[\"result_summary\"],\n            token_used=row[\"token_used\"],\n            duration_sec=row[\"duration_sec\"],\n            started_at=row[\"started_at\"],\n            finished_at=row[\"finished_at\"],\n            retry_count=row[\"retry_count\"],\n            verify_cmd=row[\"verify_cmd\"],\n            metadata=parsed_metadata,\n        )\n\n    def _load_stage_metadata(self, stage_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        stage = self.get_stage(stage_id)\n        if stage is None:\n            return {}\n        return dict(stage.metadata)\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.started","item":{"id":"item_19","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' harness/stage_executor.py\"","aggregated_output":"","exit_code":null,"status":"in_progress"}}
{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_19","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' harness/stage_executor.py\"","aggregated_output":"\"\"\"CLI-backed stage execution.\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Awaitable, Callable, Optional\n\nfrom config import settings\nfrom harness.chat2api_client import Chat2APIClient\nfrom harness.model_registry import ModelRegistry\nfrom harness.models import PermissionDecision, StageExecutionResult, StageRecord, StageStatus\nfrom harness.permission_gate import PermissionGate\n\n\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass CommandExecution:\n    returncode: int\n    stdout: str\n    stderr: str\n\n\nRunner = Callable[[list[str], str], Awaitable[CommandExecution]]\n\n\nclass StageExecutionError(RuntimeError):\n    \"\"\"Base stage execution error.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass QuotaExhaustedError(StageExecutionError):\n    \"\"\"Raised when the underlying provider reports quota exhaustion.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, provider: str, message: str, partial_output: str = \"\") -> None:\n        super().__init__(message)\n        self.provider = provider\n        self.partial_output = partial_output\n\n\nclass PermissionBlockedError(StageExecutionError):\n    \"\"\"Raised when an action is blocked by the permission gate.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, command: str, decision: PermissionDecision) -> None:\n        super().__init__(decision.reason)\n        self.command = command\n        self.decision = decision\n\n\nclass StageExecutor:\n    \"\"\"Execute planned stages via CLI backends or lightweight API calls.\"\"\"\n\n    APPROVED_DECISIONS = {\"auto_approved\", \"model_approved\", \"user_approved\"}\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        *,\n        chat_client: Optional[Chat2APIClient] = None,\n        registry: Optional[ModelRegistry] = None,\n        permission_gate: Optional[PermissionGate] = None,\n        runner: Optional[Runner] = None,\n        codex_tier: str = settings.CODEX_TIER,\n    ) -> None:\n        self.chat_client = chat_client or Chat2APIClient()\n        self.registry = registry or ModelRegistry()\n        self.permission_gate = permission_gate or PermissionGate()\n        self.runner = runner or self._run_command\n        self.codex_tier = codex_tier\n\n    async def execute(self, stage: StageRecord, handoff_doc_path: str, working_dir: str | None) -> StageExecutionResult:\n        started_at = time.time()\n        prompt = Path(handoff_doc_path).read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        provider = stage.assigned_provider or self._infer_provider(stage.assigned_model or \"\")\n        cwd = working_dir or \".\"\n\n        if provider == \"chat2api\":\n            raw_output = await asyncio.to_thread(\n                self.chat_client.chat,\n                model=stage.assigned_model or \"\",\n                prompt=prompt,\n            )\n            duration = time.time() - started_at\n            return StageExecutionResult(\n                stage_id=stage.stage_id,\n                status=StageStatus.DONE,\n                raw_output=raw_output,\n                summary=self._summarize(raw_output),\n                duration_sec=duration,\n            )\n\n        if provider == \"github\":\n            return await self._run_github_ops(stage, prompt, cwd, started_at)\n\n        command = self.build_command(stage, prompt, cwd)\n        completed = await self._run_checked_command(\n            command,\n            cwd,\n            context=f\"stage={stage.stage_type} role={stage.model_role}\",\n        )\n        combined_output = completed.stdout.strip() or completed.stderr.strip()\n\n        if self._looks_like_quota_exhaustion(completed.stdout, completed.stderr, completed.returncode):\n            raise QuotaExhaustedError(provider or \"unknown\", combined_output, partial_output=completed.stdout)\n        if completed.returncode != 0:\n            raise StageExecutionError(combined_output or f\"Stage failed with exit code {completed.returncode}\")\n\n        duration = time.time() - started_at\n        files_changed = self._list_changed_files(cwd)\n        raw_output = self._normalize_output(completed.stdout, stage.assigned_provider)\n        return StageExecutionResult(\n            stage_id=stage.stage_id,\n            status=StageStatus.DONE,\n            raw_output=raw_output,\n            summary=self._summarize(raw_output),\n            files_changed=files_changed,\n            duration_sec=duration,\n            token_used=self._extract_token_usage(completed.stdout, stage.assigned_provider),\n        )\n\n    def build_command(self, stage: StageRecord, prompt: str, working_dir: str) -> list[str]:\n        provider = stage.assigned_provider or self._infer_provider(stage.assigned_model or \"\")\n        model = stage.assigned_model or \"\"\n        cli_model = self.registry.cli_model_id(model)\n        if provider == \"claude\":\n            return [\n                \"claude\",\n                \"-p\",\n                \"--output-format\",\n                \"stream-json\",\n                \"--model\",\n                cli_model,\n                \"--allowedTools\",\n                \"Edit,Read,Bash,Grep,Glob,Write\",\n                prompt,\n            ]\n        if provider == \"codex\":\n            cmd = [\n                \"codex\",\n                \"exec\",\n                \"--json\",\n                \"-C\",\n                working_dir,\n                \"--full-auto\",\n                prompt,\n            ]\n            return cmd\n        if provider == \"google\":\n            return [\n                \"gemini\",\n                \"--model\",\n                cli_model,\n                \"-y\",   # yolo mode: auto-approve all tool calls (write_file, shell, etc.)\n                \"-p\",\n                prompt,\n            ]\n        raise StageExecutionError(f\"Unsupported provider for CLI execution: {provider}\")\n\n    async def run_verify_cmd(self, verify_cmd: str, working_dir: str | None) -> CommandExecution:\n        return await self._run_checked_command(\n            [\"bash\", \"-lc\", verify_cmd],\n            working_dir or \".\",\n            context=\"verification command\",\n            display_command=verify_cmd,\n        )\n\n    async def _run_github_ops(\n        self,\n        stage: StageRecord,\n        prompt: str,\n        working_dir: str,\n        started_at: float,\n    ) -> StageExecutionResult:\n        commands = [str(item) for item in stage.metadata.get(\"commands\", [])]\n        if not commands:\n            summary = \"GitHub stage had no commands configured.\"\n            return StageExecutionResult(\n                stage_id=stage.stage_id,\n                status=StageStatus.DONE,\n                raw_output=summary,\n                summary=summary,\n                duration_sec=time.time() - started_at,\n            )\n\n        commit_summary = self._extract_commit_summary(prompt, stage)\n\n        outputs: list[str] = []\n        for raw_command in commands:\n            rendered = raw_command.format(\n                summary=commit_summary,\n                stage_type=stage.stage_type,\n                working_dir=working_dir,\n                prompt=prompt,\n                task_id=stage.task_id,\n            )\n            completed = await self._run_checked_command(\n                [\"bash\", \"-lc\", rendered],\n                working_dir,\n                context=f\"github_ops stage={stage.stage_type}\",\n                display_command=rendered,\n            )\n            if completed.returncode != 0:\n                raise StageExecutionError(completed.stderr.strip() or completed.stdout.strip() or rendered)\n            output = completed.stdout.strip() or completed.stderr.strip() or f\"Executed: {rendered}\"\n            outputs.append(output)\n\n        raw_output = \"\\n\".join(outputs).strip()\n        return StageExecutionResult(\n            stage_id=stage.stage_id,\n            status=StageStatus.DONE,\n            raw_output=raw_output,\n            summary=self._summarize(raw_output),\n            files_changed=self._list_changed_files(working_dir),\n            duration_sec=time.time() - started_at,\n        )\n\n    async def _run_checked_command(\n        self,\n        command: list[str],\n        cwd: str,\n        *,\n        context: str,\n        display_command: Optional[str] = None,\n    ) -> CommandExecution:\n        command_text = display_command or self._display_command(command)\n        decision = await self.permission_gate.decide(\n            command_text,\n            context=context,\n            available_models=self._safe_list_available_models(),\n        )\n        if decision.decision not in self.APPROVED_DECISIONS:\n            raise PermissionBlockedError(command_text, decision)\n        return await self.runner(command, cwd)\n\n    async def _run_command(self, command: list[str], cwd: str) -> CommandExecution:\n        process = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n            *command,\n            cwd=cwd,\n            stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n        )\n        stdout, stderr = await process.communicate()\n        return CommandExecution(\n            returncode=process.returncode,\n            stdout=stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\"),\n            stderr=stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\"),\n        )\n\n    def _normalize_output(self, stdout: str, provider: Optional[str]) -> str:\n        text = stdout.strip()\n        if provider != \"codex\" or not text:\n            return text\n        try:\n            parsed = json.loads(text)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n            return text\n        if isinstance(parsed, dict):\n            if \"output_text\" in parsed and isinstance(parsed[\"output_text\"], str):\n                return parsed[\"output_text\"]\n            if \"message\" in parsed and isinstance(parsed[\"message\"], str):\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
{"type":"item.started","item":{"id":"item_20","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' harness/quota_router.py\"","aggregated_output":"","exit_code":null,"status":"in_progress"}}
{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_20","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' harness/quota_router.py\"","aggregated_output":"\"\"\"Quota-aware model routing.\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nfrom config import settings\nfrom harness.chat2api_client import Chat2APIClient\nfrom harness.model_registry import ModelRegistry\nfrom harness.models import ModelChoice, StageRecord, TaskRecord\nfrom models.quota_manager import QuotaManager, QuotaSnapshot\n\n\nclass AllQuotaExhaustedError(RuntimeError):\n    \"\"\"Raised when no configured candidate model is currently usable.\"\"\"\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass EligibleCandidate:\n    model: str\n    provider: str\n    role_index: int\n    snapshot: Optional[QuotaSnapshot] = None\n\n\nclass QuotaRouter:\n    \"\"\"Select a model using role preferences, availability, and quota heuristics.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        *,\n        registry: Optional[ModelRegistry] = None,\n        quota_manager: Optional[QuotaManager] = None,\n        chat_client: Optional[Chat2APIClient] = None,\n        min_remaining_pct: int = settings.HARNESS_MIN_QUOTA_PCT,\n        enable_llm_selector: bool = True,\n    ) -> None:\n        self.registry = registry or ModelRegistry()\n        self.quota_manager = quota_manager or QuotaManager()\n        self.chat_client = chat_client or Chat2APIClient()\n        self.min_remaining_pct = min_remaining_pct\n        self.enable_llm_selector = enable_llm_selector\n\n    def models_for_role(self, role: str) -> list[str]:\n        return self.registry.models_for_role(role)\n\n    def get_model_info(self, model: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        return self.registry.get_info(model)\n\n    def list_available_models(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return canonical model names whose chat2api_id is currently live.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            live_ids = set(self.chat_client.list_models())\n        except Exception:\n            return self.registry.canonical_names()\n        if not live_ids:\n            return self.registry.canonical_names()\n        return self.registry.available_canonical_names(live_ids)\n\n    def can_run_stage(\n        self,\n        task: TaskRecord,\n        stage: StageRecord,\n        *,\n        quota_snapshot: Optional[list[QuotaSnapshot]] = None,\n        available_models: Optional[list[str]] = None,\n    ) -> bool:\n        try:\n            self.select_model(task, stage, quota_snapshot=quota_snapshot, available_models=available_models)\n        except AllQuotaExhaustedError:\n            return False\n        return True\n\n    def select_model(\n        self,\n        task: TaskRecord,\n        stage: StageRecord,\n        *,\n        quota_snapshot: Optional[list[QuotaSnapshot]] = None,\n        available_models: Optional[list[str]] = None,\n    ) -> ModelChoice:\n        quotas = quota_snapshot if quota_snapshot is not None else self.quota_manager.fetch_all_quotas()\n        available = set(available_models or self.list_available_models())\n        eligible = self._eligible_candidates(self.models_for_role(stage.model_role), quotas, available)\n\n        if not eligible:\n            raise AllQuotaExhaustedError(\n                f\"No eligible model for role={stage.model_role} with min_remaining={self.min_remaining_pct}%\"\n            )\n\n        if self.enable_llm_selector:\n            llm_choice = self._select_with_llm(task, stage, quotas, eligible)\n            if llm_choice is not None:\n                return llm_choice\n\n        return self._fallback_select(task, stage, eligible)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Internals\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _eligible_candidates(\n        self,\n        candidates: list[str],\n        quotas: list[QuotaSnapshot],\n        available: set[str],\n    ) -> list[EligibleCandidate]:\n        eligible: list[EligibleCandidate] = []\n        for role_index, model in enumerate(candidates):\n            if model not in available:\n                continue\n            provider = self.registry.provider(model)\n            if not provider:\n                continue\n\n            provider_snapshots = [\n                snapshot\n                for snapshot in quotas\n                if snapshot.provider.value == provider and snapshot.remaining_percent >= self.min_remaining_pct\n            ]\n            if provider_snapshots:\n                for snapshot in provider_snapshots:\n                    eligible.append(\n                        EligibleCandidate(model=model, provider=provider, role_index=role_index, snapshot=snapshot)\n                    )\n            elif provider == \"github\":\n                eligible.append(\n                    EligibleCandidate(model=model, provider=provider, role_index=role_index, snapshot=None)\n                )\n        return eligible\n\n    def _select_with_llm(\n        self,\n        task: TaskRecord,\n        stage: StageRecord,\n        quotas: list[QuotaSnapshot],\n        eligible: list[EligibleCandidate],\n    ) -> ModelChoice | None:\n        selector_model = self._pick_selector_model()\n        if selector_model is None:\n            return None\n\n        prompt = self._build_selection_prompt(task, stage, quotas, eligible)\n        try:\n            raw = self.chat_client.chat(\n                model=selector_model,\n                prompt=prompt,\n                system=(\n                    \"You are code-orchestra's model router. \"\n                    \"Return compact JSON with fields model, provider, account, reason.\"\n                ),\n            )\n            parsed = json.loads(raw)\n        except Exception:\n            return None\n\n        chosen_model = parsed.get(\"model\")\n        if not isinstance(chosen_model, str):\n            return None\n\n        # LLM might respond with any alias — resolve to canonical\n        chosen_model = self.registry.resolve(chosen_model)\n\n        selected = self._match_llm_choice(chosen_model, parsed.get(\"account\"), eligible)\n        if selected is None:\n            return None\n\n        provider = selected.provider\n        snapshot = selected.snapshot\n        if snapshot is not None:\n            requested_account = parsed.get(\"account\")\n            if isinstance(requested_account, str) and requested_account and requested_account != snapshot.email:\n                alternative = next(\n                    (\n                        quota\n                        for quota in quotas\n                        if quota.provider.value == provider\n                        and quota.email == requested_account\n                        and quota.remaining_percent >= self.min_remaining_pct\n                    ),\n                    None,\n                )\n                if alternative is not None:\n                    snapshot = alternative\n\n        return ModelChoice(\n            model=chosen_model,\n            provider=provider,\n            account_email=snapshot.email if snapshot is not None else None,\n            reason=str(parsed.get(\"reason\") or \"llm_selector\"),\n        )\n\n    def _fallback_select(\n        self,\n        task: TaskRecord,\n        stage: StageRecord,\n        eligible: list[EligibleCandidate],\n    ) -> ModelChoice:\n        best_choice: ModelChoice | None = None\n        best_score: float | None = None\n\n        for candidate in eligible:\n            role_bonus = max(0, 40 - candidate.role_index * 10)\n            model = candidate.model\n            provider = candidate.provider\n            snapshot = candidate.snapshot\n            if snapshot is not None:\n                total_score = role_bonus + self._score_snapshot(snapshot)\n                choice = ModelChoice(\n                    model=model,\n                    provider=provider,\n                    account_email=snapshot.email,\n                    reason=(\n                        f\"fallback: role={stage.model_role}, complexity={task.complexity}, \"\n                        f\"remaining={snapshot.remaining_percent}%, \"\n                        f\"reset_in={snapshot.time_until_reset_hours:.1f}h\"\n                    ),\n                )\n            else:\n                total_score = role_bonus + self._score_provider_without_snapshot(provider)\n                choice = ModelChoice(\n                    model=model,\n                    provider=provider,\n                    account_email=None,\n                    reason=f\"fallback: role={stage.model_role}, complexity={task.complexity}, provider={provider}\",\n                )\n\n            if best_score is None or total_score > best_score:\n                best_score = total_score\n                best_choice = choice\n\n        if best_choice is None:\n            raise AllQuotaExhaustedError(\n                f\"No eligible model for role={stage.model_role} with min_remaining={self.min_remaining_pct}%\"\n            )\n        return best_choice\n\n    def _pick_selector_model(self) -> str | None:\n        \"\"\"Pick cheapest available model for LLM selection. Returns chat2api_id.\"\"\"\n        preferred = (\"gpt-4o\", \"claude-haiku-4-5\", \"gemini-3.1-pro\", \"gpt-5.4-codex\")\n        available = set(self.list_available_models())\n        for model in preferred:\n            if model in available:\n                return self.registry.chat2api_id(model)\n        return None\n\n    def _build_selection_prompt(\n        self,\n        task: TaskRecord,\n        stage: StageRecord,\n        quotas: list[QuotaSnapshot],\n        eligible: list[EligibleCandidate],\n    ) -> str:\n        lines = [\n            f\"Task: {task.title}\",\n            f\"Description: {task.description}\",\n            f\"Complexity: {task.complexity}\",\n            f\"Stage: {stage.stage_type} ({stage.model_role})\",\n            \"\",\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
{"type":"item.started","item":{"id":"item_21","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '260,520p' harness/quota_router.py\"","aggregated_output":"","exit_code":null,"status":"in_progress"}}
{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_21","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '260,520p' harness/quota_router.py\"","aggregated_output":"            \"\",\n            \"Eligible models:\",\n        ]\n        for candidate in eligible:\n            if candidate.snapshot is None:\n                lines.append(f\"- {candidate.model} | provider={candidate.provider} | remaining=n/a\")\n            else:\n                lines.append(\n                    f\"- {candidate.model} | provider={candidate.provider} | account={candidate.snapshot.email} | \"\n                    f\"remaining={candidate.snapshot.remaining_percent}% | reset_in={candidate.snapshot.time_until_reset_hours:.1f}h\"\n                )\n        lines.extend(\n            [\n                \"\",\n                \"Prefer use-it-or-lose-it quota, but keep role fit.\",\n                'Respond as JSON: {\"model\": \"...\", \"provider\": \"...\", \"account\": \"...\", \"reason\": \"...\"}',\n            ]\n        )\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n    def _match_llm_choice(\n        self,\n        model: str,\n        account: object,\n        eligible: list[EligibleCandidate],\n    ) -> EligibleCandidate | None:\n        model_candidates = [c for c in eligible if c.model == model]\n        if not model_candidates:\n            return None\n        requested_account = account if isinstance(account, str) else None\n        if requested_account:\n            exact = next(\n                (c for c in model_candidates if c.snapshot is not None and c.snapshot.email == requested_account),\n                None,\n            )\n            if exact is not None:\n                return exact\n        return max(\n            model_candidates,\n            key=lambda c: self._score_snapshot(c.snapshot) if c.snapshot is not None else -1.0,\n        )\n\n    def _score_snapshot(self, snapshot: QuotaSnapshot) -> float:\n        scorer = getattr(self.quota_manager, \"_score_account\", None)\n        if callable(scorer):\n            try:\n                return float(scorer(snapshot, False))\n            except TypeError:\n                return float(scorer(snapshot))\n        waste_bonus = 0.0\n        if snapshot.time_until_reset_hours <= 6:\n            waste_bonus = snapshot.remaining_percent * (6 - max(snapshot.time_until_reset_hours, 0)) / 6\n        safety_bonus = 15.0 if snapshot.remaining_percent >= 40 else 0.0\n        return snapshot.remaining_percent + waste_bonus + safety_bonus\n\n    def _score_provider_without_snapshot(self, provider: str) -> float:\n        if provider == \"github\":\n            return 85.0\n        if provider == \"claude\":\n            return 70.0\n        return 50.0\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
{"type":"item.started","item":{"id":"item_22","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '260,520p' harness/stage_executor.py\"","aggregated_output":"","exit_code":null,"status":"in_progress"}}
{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_22","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '260,520p' harness/stage_executor.py\"","aggregated_output":"            if \"message\" in parsed and isinstance(parsed[\"message\"], str):\n                return parsed[\"message\"]\n        return text\n\n    def _extract_token_usage(self, stdout: str, provider: Optional[str]) -> int:\n        text = stdout.strip()\n        if provider != \"codex\" or not text:\n            return 0\n        try:\n            parsed = json.loads(text)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n            return 0\n        return max(0, self._find_token_total(parsed))\n\n    def _summarize(self, text: str) -> str:\n        cleaned = \" \".join(text.split())\n        if len(cleaned) <= 280:\n            return cleaned\n        return cleaned[:277] + \"...\"\n\n    def _extract_commit_summary(self, prompt: str, stage: StageRecord) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build a short commit message from the handoff doc.\"\"\"\n        # Look for \"# Task: <title>\" in the handoff markdown\n        for line in prompt.splitlines():\n            stripped = line.strip()\n            if stripped.startswith(\"# Task:\") or stripped.startswith(\"# \"):\n                title = stripped.lstrip(\"# \").removeprefix(\"Task:\").strip()\n                if title:\n                    return title[:120]\n        return f\"task {stage.task_id}\"\n\n    def _infer_provider(self, model: str) -> str:\n        if model.startswith(\"claude-\"):\n            return \"claude\"\n        if \"codex\" in model:\n            return \"codex\"\n        if model.startswith(\"gemini-\"):\n            return \"google\"\n        if model.startswith(\"gpt-4o\") or model.startswith(\"gpt-4.1\"):\n            return \"github\"\n        return \"\"\n\n    def _looks_like_quota_exhaustion(self, stdout: str, stderr: str, returncode: int = 1) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Detect provider quota/rate-limit errors.\n\n        Only triggers on non-zero exit codes to avoid false positives when\n        the model output itself discusses quotas (e.g. planning a quota feature).\n        \"\"\"\n        if returncode == 0:\n            return False\n        text = f\"{stdout}\\n{stderr}\".lower()\n        return any(token in text for token in (\"rate limit\", \"limit reached\", \"quota exceeded\", \"quota exhausted\", \"rate_limit_exceeded\"))\n\n    def _list_changed_files(self, working_dir: str) -> list[str]:\n        try:\n            completed = subprocess.run(\n                [\"git\", \"diff\", \"--name-only\"],\n                cwd=working_dir,\n                capture_output=True,\n                text=True,\n                check=False,\n            )\n        except OSError:\n            return []\n        if completed.returncode != 0:\n            return []\n        return [line for line in completed.stdout.splitlines() if line.strip()]\n\n    def _safe_list_available_models(self) -> list[str] | None:\n        try:\n            return self.chat_client.list_models()\n        except Exception:\n            return None\n\n    def _display_command(self, command: list[str]) -> str:\n        return subprocess.list2cmdline(command)\n\n    def _find_token_total(self, payload: object) -> int:\n        if isinstance(payload, dict):\n            for key in (\"total_tokens\", \"token_used\"):\n                value = self._coerce_int(payload.get(key))\n                if value is not None:\n                    return value\n\n            usage = payload.get(\"usage\")\n            if isinstance(usage, dict):\n                total = self._coerce_int(usage.get(\"total_tokens\"))\n                if total is not None:\n                    return total\n                input_tokens = self._coerce_int(usage.get(\"input_tokens\")) or 0\n                output_tokens = self._coerce_int(usage.get(\"output_tokens\")) or 0\n                if input_tokens or output_tokens:\n                    return input_tokens + output_tokens\n\n            for value in payload.values():\n                nested = self._find_token_total(value)\n                if nested:\n                    return nested\n\n        if isinstance(payload, list):\n            totals = [self._find_token_total(item) for item in payload]\n            return sum(total for total in totals if total)\n\n        return 0\n\n    def _coerce_int(self, value: object) -> int | None:\n        if value is None:\n            return None\n        try:\n            return int(value)\n        except (TypeError, ValueError):\n            return None\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_23","type":"agent_message","text":"I’ve mapped the current implementation: the runtime already supports stage-level `assigned_model`/`assigned_provider`, and `_run_stage()` already bypasses routing when those are set. The missing work is concentrated in intake planning, stage persistence defaults, CLI parsing, and normalization of user-provided model names."}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_27","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' scripts/orchestra_cli.py\"","aggregated_output":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Interactive CLI for running multi-agent orchestrator workflows.\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport re\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nimport uuid\nimport yaml\n\n# Fix path to allow importing from the project root if run from anywhere\nsys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent.absolute()))\n\nfrom common.acp_client import ACPClient\nfrom common.models import Message, MessagePart, RunStatus\nfrom scripts.check_server_status import check_all_servers, StatusReport\nfrom orchestrator.pipeline import PipelineDefinition, PipelineExecutor, PipelineError\nfrom orchestrator.stage import StageDefinition, StageResult\n\n# Setup minimal logging, only warning+ so it doesn't clutter CLI\nlogging.basicConfig(level=logging.WARNING, format=\"%(message)s\")\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\ndef hide_code_blocks(text: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Replaces markdown code blocks and diffs with a hidden placeholder.\"\"\"\n    if not text:\n        return \"\"\n    # Regex to match ```language \\n code... \\n ```\n    pattern = re.compile(r\"```[a-zA-Z0-9_-]*\\n[\\s\\S]*?\\n```\", re.MULTILINE)\n    return pattern.sub(\"\\n```\\n[CODE DIFF/BLOCK HIDDEN]\\n```\\n\", text)\n    \n\nclass CLIExecutor(PipelineExecutor):\n    \"\"\"Custom executor that streams updates to the CLI interface and routes to ACP services.\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, pipeline_def: PipelineDefinition, config_path: str):\n        super().__init__(pipeline_def)\n        self.session_id = uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]\n        self.clients: dict[str, ACPClient] = {}\n        \n        with open(config_path, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as handle:\n            data = yaml.safe_load(handle) or {}\n            for name, info in data.get(\"agents\", {}).items():\n                self.clients[name] = ACPClient(info[\"base_url\"])\n                \n    async def cleanup(self):\n        import asyncio\n        await asyncio.gather(*(client.close() for client in self.clients.values()), return_exceptions=True)\n\n    async def _invoke_model(self, stage_def: StageDefinition, prompt: str) -> str:\n        agent_name = stage_def.metadata.get(\"agent\")\n        if not agent_name:\n            return await super()._invoke_model(stage_def, prompt)\n            \n        client = self.clients.get(agent_name)\n        if not client:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Unknown agent '{agent_name}' configured in metadata for stage {stage_def.stage_id}\")\n            \n        message = Message(\n            role=\"user\",\n            parts=[MessagePart(content=prompt)]\n        )\n        \n        run = await client.create_run(agent_name, [message], session_id=self.session_id)\n        if run.status != RunStatus.COMPLETED:\n            raise RuntimeError(f\"Agent {agent_name} failed: {run.error}\")\n            \n        if not run.output_messages:\n            return \"\"\n        return run.output_messages[0].text\n    \n    async def _execute_stage(self, stage_def: StageDefinition) -> StageResult:\n        \"\"\"Override to provide CLI feedback before and after the stage.\"\"\"\n        print(f\"\\n========================================================\")\n        print(f\"🔄 CURRENT STEP: {stage_def.name} ({stage_def.stage_id})\")\n        print(f\"🤖 Agent Model:  {stage_def.model}\")\n        print(f\"========================================================\")\n        \n        # Prepare prompt to show user what is being asked\n        prompt = self._prepare_prompt(stage_def)\n        print(\"\\n[Input] -> Sending to Agent:\")\n        print(\"-\" * 40)\n        # We don't hide code blocks in prompt for context, but we can limit size\n        short_prompt = prompt if len(prompt) < 1000 else prompt[:500] + \"\\n...[TRUNCATED FOR DISPLAY]...\" + prompt[-500:]\n        print(short_prompt)\n        print(\"-\" * 40)\n        print(\"\\n⏳ Waiting for agent response...\\n\")\n        \n        start_time = time.time()\n        try:\n            output = await self._invoke_model(stage_def, prompt)\n            execution_time = time.time() - start_time\n            \n            print(f\"✅ Agent responded in {execution_time:.1f}s\")\n            print(\"\\n[Output] <- Received from Agent:\")\n            print(\"-\" * 40)\n            \n            # Hide code blocks for clean UI\n            clean_output = hide_code_blocks(output)\n            print(clean_output)\n            print(\"-\" * 40)\n            \n            return StageResult(\n                stage_id=stage_def.stage_id,\n                status=\"success\",\n                output=output,\n                raw_output=output,\n                execution_time=execution_time,\n                model_used=stage_def.model,\n            )\n            \n        except Exception as exc:\n            execution_time = time.time() - start_time\n            print(f\"❌ Stage failed after {execution_time:.1f}s: {exc}\")\n            raise  # Let the base class intercept it and handle fail_fast\n\n\nasync def ensure_servers_running(config_path: str, timeout: float = 3.0) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if required servers are healthy. Return True if all ok.\"\"\"\n    print(\"🔍 Checking ACP Server Status...\")\n    report = await check_all_servers(config_path, timeout)\n    if report.unhealthy_count == 0:\n        print(f\"✅ All {report.total_servers} servers are healthy!\")\n        return True\n    \n    print(\"\\n❌ SOME SERVERS ARE UNREACHABLE!\")\n    for server in report.servers:\n        if not server.healthy:\n            err = f\" ({server.error})\" if server.error else \"\"\n            print(f\"  - {server.base_url} is DOWN{err}\")\n    \n    print(\"\\nPlease start the required servers (e.g. using scripts/run_*_server.py).\")\n    return False\n\n\ndef get_available_workflows() -> list[Path]:\n    \"\"\"Return a list of workflow YAML files.\"\"\"\n    workflows_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent / \"workflows\"\n    if not workflows_dir.exists():\n        return []\n    return sorted(workflows_dir.glob(\"*.yaml\"))\n\n\nasync def main_async(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\n    # 1. Check Servers\n    servers_ok = await ensure_servers_running(args.config, args.timeout)\n    if not servers_ok:\n        sys.exit(1)\n        \n    print(\"\\n\" + \"=\"*60)\n    print(\"               ORCHESTRA MULTI-AGENT CLI\")\n    print(\"=\"*60 + \"\\n\")\n    \n    # 2. Select Workflow\n    workflows = get_available_workflows()\n    if not workflows:\n        print(\"❌ No workflows found in the 'workflows/' directory.\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n        \n    workflow_path = None\n    if args.workflow:\n        workflow_path = Path(args.workflow)\n        if not workflow_path.exists():\n            print(f\"❌ Workflow {workflow_path} not found.\")\n            sys.exit(1)\n    else:\n        print(\"Available Workflows:\")\n        for idx, wf in enumerate(workflows):\n            print(f\"  [{idx + 1}] {wf.name}\")\n            \n        while True:\n            try:\n                choice = input(\"\\nSelect a workflow (1-%d): \" % len(workflows))\n                idx = int(choice.strip()) - 1\n                if 0 <= idx < len(workflows):\n                    workflow_path = workflows[idx]\n                    break\n                else:\n                    print(\"Invalid choice.\")\n            except ValueError:\n                print(\"Please enter a number.\")\n            except KeyboardInterrupt:\n                print(\"\\nCanceled by user.\")\n                sys.exit(0)\n                \n    print(f\"\\n✅ Selected workflow: {workflow_path.name}\")\n    \n    # Load pipeline\n    try:\n        pipeline_def = PipelineDefinition(workflow_path)\n    except PipelineError as e:\n        print(f\"❌ Failed to load pipeline: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n        \n    # 3. Get Task\n    task_description = args.task\n    if not task_description:\n        print(\"\\nEnter task description (press Enter twice to finish):\")\n        lines = []\n        while True:\n            try:\n                line = input()\n                if not line and (not lines or not lines[-1]):\n                    break\n                lines.append(line)\n            except KeyboardInterrupt:\n                print(\"\\nCanceled by user.\")\n                sys.exit(0)\n        task_description = \"\\n\".join(lines).strip()\n        \n    if not task_description:\n        print(\"❌ Task description cannot be empty.\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # 4. Execute Pipeline\n    print(\"\\n🚀 Starting Pipeline Execution...\")\n    executor = CLIExecutor(pipeline_def, args.config)\n    \n    try:\n        # Also map user_task so it's available for workflows explicitly requesting it\n        initial_state = {\"user_task\": task_description}\n        results = await executor.execute(task_description, initial_state=initial_state)\n        \n        print(\"\\n\" + \"=\"*60)\n        print(\"🎉 PIPELINE EXECUTION COMPLETED\")\n        print(\"=\"*60)\n        for stage_id, result in results.items():\n            icon = \"✅\" if result.status == \"success\" else \"❌\"\n            print(f\"{icon} Stage '{stage_id}': {result.status.upper()} ({result.execution_time:.1f}s)\")\n            \n        # Check for absolute failure\n        if any(r.status == \"failed\" for r in results.values()):\n            print(\"\\n❌ Pipeline completed with ERRORS.\")\n            sys.exit(1)\n            \n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"\\n❌ Pipeline execution failed due to an error: {e}\")\n        import traceback\n        traceback.print_exc()\n        sys.exit(1)\n    finally:\n        await executor.cleanup()\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Interactive Multi-Agent Orchestrator CLI\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--config\", default=\"config/agents.yaml\", help=\"Path to agents.yaml\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--timeout\", type=float, default=3.0, help=\"Per-server health check timeout in seconds\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--workflow\", help=\"Path to a specific workflow YAML, bypasses selection\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--task\", help=\"Initial task description, bypasses input dialog\")\n    \n    args = parser.parse_args()\n    \n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_28","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' tests/harness/test_daemon.py\"","aggregated_output":"import signal\n\nfrom harness.daemon import DaemonManager\nfrom harness.main import build_parser\n\n\nclass FakeProcess:\n    def __init__(self, pid: int) -> None:\n        self.pid = pid\n\n\ndef test_daemon_manager_start_stop_and_status(tmp_path) -> None:\n    running = {4321: False}\n    launched = {}\n    signals = []\n\n    def popen_factory(command, **kwargs):\n        launched[\"command\"] = command\n        launched[\"kwargs\"] = kwargs\n        running[4321] = True\n        return FakeProcess(4321)\n\n    def is_process_running(pid: int) -> bool:\n        return running.get(pid, False)\n\n    def signal_process(pid: int, sig: int) -> None:\n        signals.append((pid, sig))\n        running[pid] = False\n\n    manager = DaemonManager(\n        pid_file=tmp_path / \"orchestra.pid\",\n        state_file=tmp_path / \"orchestra.json\",\n        log_file=tmp_path / \"orchestra.log\",\n        cwd=tmp_path,\n        popen_factory=popen_factory,\n        is_process_running=is_process_running,\n        signal_process=signal_process,\n        sleep_fn=lambda _seconds: None,\n    )\n\n    started = manager.start(poll_interval_sec=7)\n    stopped = manager.stop(timeout_sec=1.0)\n\n    assert started[\"running\"] is True\n    assert started[\"action\"] == \"started\"\n    assert launched[\"command\"][-2:] == [\"--poll-interval\", \"7\"]\n    assert launched[\"kwargs\"][\"start_new_session\"] is True\n    assert signals == [(4321, signal.SIGTERM)]\n    assert stopped[\"action\"] == \"stopped\"\n    assert stopped[\"graceful\"] is True\n    assert not (tmp_path / \"orchestra.pid\").exists()\n\n\ndef test_daemon_status_cleans_stale_pid_file(tmp_path) -> None:\n    pid_file = tmp_path / \"orchestra.pid\"\n    state_file = tmp_path / \"orchestra.json\"\n    pid_file.write_text(\"9999\\n\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    state_file.write_text('{\"pid\": 9999, \"command\": [\"python\"]}', encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n    manager = DaemonManager(\n        pid_file=pid_file,\n        state_file=state_file,\n        log_file=tmp_path / \"orchestra.log\",\n        is_process_running=lambda _pid: False,\n    )\n\n    status = manager.status()\n\n    assert status[\"running\"] is False\n    assert status[\"stale_pid\"] is True\n    assert not pid_file.exists()\n    assert not state_file.exists()\n\n\ndef test_parser_supports_daemon_and_dashboard_commands() -> None:\n    parser = build_parser()\n\n    dashboard = parser.parse_args([\"dashboard\", \"--recent-events\", \"5\"])\n    daemon = parser.parse_args([\"daemon\", \"start\", \"--poll-interval\", \"3\"])\n\n    assert dashboard.command == \"dashboard\"\n    assert dashboard.recent_events == 5\n    assert daemon.command == \"daemon\"\n    assert daemon.daemon_command == \"start\"\n    assert daemon.poll_interval == 3\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.started","item":{"id":"item_30","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,240p' artifacts/ff4983b36ba94887bc2f2a1d938eadba/01_plan_handoff.md\"","aggregated_output":"","exit_code":null,"status":"in_progress"}}
{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_30","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,240p' artifacts/ff4983b36ba94887bc2f2a1d938eadba/01_plan_handoff.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Handoff: bootstrap -> plan\n## Task\nfeat: user-specified pipeline model assignments\n\n## Description\nAdd a user-specified pipeline feature to the orchestra harness.\n\nCurrently the QuotaRouter intelligently selects which model handles each stage.\nAdd an option for users to explicitly specify which model handles each stage when submitting a task.\n\nRequirements:\n1. PlannedStage should accept optional assigned_model and assigned_provider fields\n2. TaskQueue.save_stages should persist these pre-assignments\n3. Harness._run_stage should skip QuotaRouter when model is pre-assigned (this already works)\n4. IntakeAgent.plan_task should accept an optional model_overrides dict mapping stage_type to (model, provider)\n5. CLI submit command should accept --model-override flags like: --model-override \"code=gemini-3.1-pro\" --model-override \"review=gpt-5.4-codex\"\n6. ModelRegistry.resolve() should be used to normalize user-provided model names (aliases supported)\n\nThe working directory is /home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra.\nTest command: python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v\n\n\n## Goal\nUsers can specify per-stage model assignments via CLI and API, with alias resolution\n\n## Context\n- Complexity: medium\n- Current Stage: plan (planner)\n- Working Directory: /home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra\n\n## Current Stage Instructions\nExecute the `plan` stage and leave a concise handoff for the next stage.\n\n## Verification\nRun: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v`\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
{"type":"item.started","item":{"id":"item_31","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,240p' artifacts/83b2ef3d77cf4f508fdea0bd372794e7/01_plan_output.md\"","aggregated_output":"","exit_code":null,"status":"in_progress"}}
{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_31","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,240p' artifacts/83b2ef3d77cf4f508fdea0bd372794e7/01_plan_output.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Output: plan\n## Summary\n本阶段只做实现规划，不改业务代码。当前 `tests/harness/` 基线已验证通过: `21 passed`.\n\n## Implementation Plan\n1. Codex 多账号轮换\n- 修改 [harness/quota_router.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/quota_router.py)，把 `codex` 候选从“按 provider 取一个最佳 snapshot”改成“按账号展开后逐个打分”，继续复用 `QuotaManager._score_account()` 的 use-it-or-lose-it 逻辑。\n- 保持 `QuotaRouter.select_model()` / `can_run_stage()` 纯函数化，不在路由阶段切账号。\n- 在 stage 上持久化被选中的 `account_email`。优先放到 `metadata`，并补一个 `TaskQueue` 的 metadata 更新接口，避免为此扩表。\n- 在 [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py) 的执行路径里增加 Codex 账号激活步骤，仅当 `assigned_provider == \"codex\"` 且目标账号与 `models.codex.account.get_active_email()` 不同时调用 `set_active_account()`。\n\n2. daemon 后台模式\n- 以 [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py) 作为管理入口，新增 `daemon start|stop|status` 和一个内部 worker 子命令。\n- 新增独立 daemon helper 模块，负责 PID 文件、metadata 文件、存活检测、stale PID 清理和后台拉起。\n- 后台运行优先走可测试的 `subprocess.Popen(..., start_new_session=True)` / nohup 风格实现，不把 systemd 作为单元测试依赖。\n- 给 `run_forever()` 增加 `SIGTERM` 可控退出路径，最好通过 shutdown flag / `asyncio.Event` 做出可测试 seam。\n\n3. logging / telemetry / dashboard\n- 新增统一 logging 配置，CLI 和 daemon 启动时共用。\n- 在 harness runtime 发出结构化 JSONL stage 事件: `stage_started`、`stage_succeeded`、`stage_retry`、`stage_paused_quota`、`stage_paused_permission`、`stage_failed`、`task_finished`。\n- `token_used` 和 `duration_sec` 继续复用现有表字段；在 [harness/stage_executor.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/stage_executor.py) 里对 Codex JSON 输出做轻量 token 解析，拿不到时保持 `0`。\n- 新增 `dashboard` 命令，聚合 tasks/stages、quota_events、permission_requests、daemon 状态和最近 JSONL 事件，输出当前概览。\n\n## Key Constraints\n- 不要让 quota 检查触发全局 Codex auth 切换。\n- 不要把 daemon 管理接到旧的 [scripts/orchestra_cli.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/scripts/orchestra_cli.py)。\n- 不要依赖真实 systemd 做测试。\n\n## Test Additions\n- 扩展 [tests/harness/test_quota_router.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/tests/harness/test_quota_router.py): 多 Codex 账号排序、`can_run_stage()` 无副作用。\n- 扩展 [tests/harness/test_main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/tests/harness/test_main.py): stage 选择账号后执行前激活。\n- 新增 daemon 测试: PID 生命周期、stale PID、graceful stop/status。\n- 新增 telemetry/dashboard 测试: JSONL 事件落盘与汇总统计。\n\n## Verification\n最终验收命令: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v`\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

artifacts/83b2ef3d77cf4f508fdea0bd372794e7/02_code_handoff.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Handoff: plan -> code\n## Task\nPhase 4: 健壮性 for code-orchestra harness\n\n## Context Gathered\n- [harness/quota_router.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/quota_router.py) 当前按 provider 取单个最佳 quota snapshot；这会丢掉 Codex 的账号级选择空间。\n- [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py) 当前只持久化 `assigned_model` / `assigned_provider`，执行前没有 Codex 账号激活，也没有 daemon / telemetry hook。\n- [harness/task_queue.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/task_queue.py) 已经有 `metadata` 字段，但缺少单独的 metadata 更新接口；这是保存 `account_email` 的最小改动点。\n- [harness/stage_executor.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/stage_executor.py) 已经返回 `duration_sec` / `token_used`，其中 `token_used` 目前始终接近未使用状态，适合在这里补 provider 输出解析。\n- [models/codex/account.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/models/codex/account.py) 已提供 `get_active_email()` 和 `set_active_account()`；账号切换副作用应放到 runtime，而不是 router。\n- 当前基线: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v` 通过，结果是 `21 passed`。\n\n## Recommended Order\n1. 先做 Codex 轮换\n- router 改成账号级候选。\n- stage metadata 持久化 `selected_account_email`。\n- `Harness._run_stage()` 在真正执行前切换 Codex 账号。\n- 先补 router 和 harness 测试，再动实现。\n\n2. 再做 daemon\n- 新建 daemon helper 模块，不要把 PID/信号逻辑塞满 `main.py`。\n- parser 增加 `daemon` 子命令和内部 worker 子命令。\n- `run_forever()` 增加可优雅退出的 stop seam。\n\n3. 最后做 telemetry 和 dashboard\n- 统一 logging 配置。\n- stage 生命周期事件写 JSONL。\n- `dashboard` 聚合 DB + daemon + recent events。\n\n## Concrete Guidance\n- `QuotaRouter.can_run_stage()` 必须保持无副作用，因为它会在 paused quota 恢复逻辑中反复调用。\n- `TaskQueue.assign_stage_model()` 很可能需要扩成同时支持 metadata patch，或者新增 `update_stage_metadata()`。\n- daemon 测试应 mock `Popen`、`os.kill(pid, 0)` 或等价存活检查，避免真实后台进程依赖。\n- `dashboard` 不必做复杂 TUI，先输出稳定 JSON/结构化文本即可。\n\n## Verification Target\n收尾时运行: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v`\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

576\t\n   577\t\n   578\tasync def _prompt_user_approval(command: str, context: str) -> bool | None:\n   579\t    if not sys.stdin.isatty():\n   580\t        return None\n   581\t [y/N]: \")\n   585\t    lowered = answer.strip().lower()\n   586\t    if lowered in {\"y\", \"yes\"}:\n   587\t        return True\n   588\t    if lowered in {\"n\", \"no\", \"\"}:\n   589\t        return False\n   590\t    return None\n   591\t\n   592\t\n   593\tdef build_cli_harness() -> Harness:\n   594\t    registry = ModelRegistry()\n   595\t    chat_client = Chat2APIClient()\n   596\t    permission_gate = PermissionGate(user_approver=_prompt_user_approval)\n   597\t    stage_executor = StageExecutor(chat_client=chat_client, registry=registry, permission_gate=permission_gate)\n   598\t    quota_router = QuotaRouter(registry=registry, chat_client=chat_client)\n   599\t    return Harness(quota_router=quota_router, stage_executor=stage_executor)\n   600\t\n   601\t\n   602\tdef build_daemon_manager() -> DaemonManager:\n   603\t    return DaemonManager(cwd=Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent)\n   604\t\n   605\t\n   606\tdef _read_multiline_description() -> str:\n   607\t    print(\"Describe the task. Press Enter twice to finish:\")\n   608\t    lines: list[str] = []\n   609\t    while True:\n   610\t        line = input()\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

canonical name → set of aliases (including the canonical name itself)\n    32\t        self._alias_to_canonical: dict[str, str] = {}\n    33\t        self._build_index()\n    34\t\n    35\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    36\t    # Public API\n    37\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    38\t\n    39\t    def resolve(self, name: str) -> str:\n    40\t        \"\"\"Resolve any name (canonical or alias) to its canonical name.\n    41\t\n    42\t        Returns the input unchanged if it is not recognised — this keeps\n    43\t        the system open to ad-hoc model names that haven't been catalogued.\n    44\t        \"\"\"\n    45\t        return self._alias_to_canonical.get(name, name)\n    46\t\n    47\t    def chat2api_id(self, name: str) -> str:\n    48\t        \"\"\"Return the chat2api model ID for *name* (canonical or alias).\"\"\"\n    49\t        canonical = self.resolve(name)\n    50\t        info = self._models.get(canonical, {})\n    51\t        return info.get(\"chat2api_id\", canonical)\n    52\t\n    53\t    def cli_model_id(self, name: str) -> str:\n    54\t        \"\"\"Return the CLI model ID for *name* (canonical or alias).\n    55\t\n    56\t        Falls back to the canonical name if no cli_model_id is configured.\n    57\t        \"\"\"\n    58\t        canonical = self.resolve(name)\n    59\t        info = self._models.get(canonical, {})\n    60\t        return info.get(\"cli_model_id\", canonical)\n    61\t\n    62\t    def provider(self, name: str) -> str:\n    63\t        \"\"\"Return the provider string for *name*.\"\"\"\n    64\t        canonical = self.resolve(name)\n    65\t        return self._models.get(canonical, {}).get(\"provider\", \"\")\n    66\t\n    67\t    def get_info(self, name: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    68\t        \"\"\"Return the full catalog entry for *name*.\"\"\"\n    69\t        canonical = self.resolve(name)\n    70\t        return dict(self._models.get(canonical, {}))\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

No production code was modified.\n\nThe requested verification baseline is currently green:\n- `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v`\n- Result: `43 passed, 3 skipped, 1 warning`\n\n## Current State\n- [`PlannedStage`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/models.py#L35) does not yet carry pre-assigned model/provider fields.\n- [`TaskQueue.save_stages()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/task_queue.py#L139) always writes `assigned_model=None` and `assigned_provider=None`, so any preplanned assignment is dropped.\n- [`Harness.submit_task()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py#L70) calls [`IntakeAgent.plan_task()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/intake.py#L87) without any override input.\n- [`Harness._run_stage()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py#L348) already skips `QuotaRouter` when both `assigned_model` and `assigned_provider` are present.\n- [`build_parser()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py#L533) and [`_main_async()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py#L634) do not expose any submit-time model override flags.\n- [`ModelRegistry.resolve()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/model_registry.py#L39) already provides the canonicalization needed for user-facing aliases.\n\n## Recommended Implementation\n1. Extend planning data\n- Add `assigned_model: Optional[str] = None` and `assigned_provider: Optional[str] = None` to [`PlannedStage`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/models.py#L35).\n- Update [`IntakeAgent.plan_task()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/intake.py#L87) to accept `model_overrides: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] | None`.\n- When constructing each `PlannedStage`, apply the matching override by `stage_type`.\n\n2. Persist pre-assignments\n- Update [`TaskQueue.save_stages()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/task_queue.py#L139) to copy `planned.assigned_model` and `planned.assigned_provider` into the stored `StageRecord`.\n\n3. Add CLI/API normalization\n- Add a harness-level path for submit-time overrides so API callers do not need to build `IntakeResult` manually.\n- Recommended shape: `Harness.submit_task(..., model_overrides: dict[str, str] | None = None)`.\n- Normalize each user model string with [`ModelRegistry.resolve()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/model_registry.py#L39), derive provider via `ModelRegistry.provider()`, and pass a normalized `dict[str, tuple[str, str]]` into intake planning.\n- Extend the `submit` CLI with repeated `--model-override` flags, for example `code=gemini` and `review=gpt-5.4`.\n\n4. Guardrails\n- Reject malformed override strings that do not look like `stage_type=model`.\n- Reject overrides whose resolved provider is empty; otherwise the stage will persist an unusable assignment.\n- Reject override stage types that are not present in the generated pipeline for the detected complexity, rather than silently ignoring them.\n\n## Important Behavioral Note\n- Explicit user pinning probably should survive quota pauses as well. Today the exhausted fallback path in [`Harness._run_stage()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py#L398) eventually calls `reset_stage_to_pending(... clear_model=True)`, which removes the assignment and allows later rerouting. If the product intent is “this stage must stay on the user-selected model,” the code stage should preserve or restore the pinned assignment there.\n\n## Test Additions\n- Extend [`tests/harness/test_intake.py`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/tests/harness/test_intake.py) to verify `plan_task(..., model_overrides=...)` stamps the matching `PlannedStage`.\n- Extend [`tests/harness/test_task_queue.py`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/tests/harness/test_task_queue.py) to verify `save_stages()` persists pre-assigned model/provider values.\n- Extend [`tests/harness/test_main.py`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/tests/harness/test_main.py) to verify `submit_task()` resolves aliases and stores canonical model/provider assignments.\n- Add a regression test proving `_run_stage()` does not call the router when a stage was pre-assigned before execution.\n- Extend [`tests/harness/test_daemon.py`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/tests/harness/test_daemon.py) or [`tests/harness/test_main.py`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/tests/harness/test_main.py) with parser coverage for repeated `--model-override`.\n\n## Verification\nRun:\n`python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v`\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_51","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,240p' artifacts/ff4983b36ba94887bc2f2a1d938eadba/02_code_handoff.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Handoff: plan -> code\n## Task\nfeat: user-specified pipeline model assignments\n\n## What To Change\n- Add optional `assigned_model` and `assigned_provider` fields to [`PlannedStage`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/models.py#L35).\n- Update [`IntakeAgent.plan_task()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/intake.py#L87) to accept `model_overrides: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] | None` and stamp matching stages during plan creation.\n- Update [`TaskQueue.save_stages()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/task_queue.py#L139) so planned assignments are persisted instead of replaced with `None`.\n- Extend [`Harness.submit_task()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py#L70) so API callers can pass per-stage overrides at submission time.\n- Extend the `submit` CLI in [`build_parser()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py#L533) and [`_main_async()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py#L634) to accept repeated `--model-override stage=model` flags.\n\n## Recommended Shape\n- Add a `registry` dependency to `Harness` so submit-time normalization can reuse the same catalog as the router/executor.\n- Parse CLI flags into `dict[str, str]`, then normalize to `dict[str, tuple[str, str]]`:\n  - canonical model = `registry.resolve(raw_model)`\n  - provider = `registry.provider(canonical_model)`\n- Pass the normalized mapping into `IntakeAgent.plan_task(...)`.\n- Keep the persisted stage assignment canonical so later inspect/status output is stable.\n\n## Edge Cases To Handle\n- Invalid flag format: fail fast with a clear `ValueError` or parser error.\n- Unknown model/provider after resolution: fail fast instead of persisting a blank provider.\n- Duplicate stage overrides: pick one behavior deliberately; erroring is safer than “last one wins”.\n- Override for a stage type not present in the generated pipeline: fail fast instead of silently ignoring it.\n\n## Existing Behavior Worth Preserving\n- [`Harness._run_stage()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py#L348) already skips routing when both assignment fields are present. Keep that path unchanged except for regression coverage.\n- Do not change router selection behavior for unpinned stages.\n\n## One Decision To Make\n- The quota retry fallback in [`Harness._run_stage()`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py#L398) eventually clears `assigned_model` / `assigned_provider`. For explicit user pins, I recommend preserving the assignment so a paused stage stays pinned after resume; otherwise the feature can silently devolve back to auto-routing after quota exhaustion.\n\n## Tests To Add First\n- [`tests/harness/test_intake.py`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/tests/harness/test_intake.py): override mapping applied to planned stages.\n- [`tests/harness/test_task_queue.py`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/tests/harness/test_task_queue.py): persisted pre-assignment round-trip.\n- [`tests/harness/test_main.py`](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/tests/harness/test_main.py): alias resolution through `submit_task()`, and router bypass when a stage is already pinned.\n- Parser coverage for repeated `--model-override`.\n\n## Verified Baseline\n- `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v`\n- Result on March 28, 2026: `43 passed, 3 skipped, 1 warning`\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
